Job Description - Director Software Engineering - Remote (2363260)
 
Director Software Engineering - Remote
-2363260

Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health optimization on a global scale. Join us to start Caring. Connecting. Growing together.

 

The Director of Architecture for Agentic AI Claims Automation will serve as the principal technical leader driving the architectural vision, solution design, and AI first innovation strategy for next generation claims automation capabilities within the Claims GCO organization. This role will define and mature the architecture for a platform that enables cross domain agentic workflows, orchestrates intelligent automation patterns, and aligns with the broader enterprise AI/ML strategy.

 

As a senior member of the Architecture job family, this individual will provide deep technical leadership across product, engineering, enterprise architecture, data science, and operations teams. They will ensure architectural consistency, guide adoption of emerging AI capabilities, and establish standards that accelerate delivery while safeguarding resilience, performance, governance, and reusability. The Director will mentor architects and engineers and act as a trusted advisor to business and technology stakeholders, influencing decisions through technical depth and strategic clarity.

 

You’ll enjoy the flexibility to work remotely * from anywhere within the U.S. as you take on some tough challenges. For all hires in the Minneapolis or Washington, D.C. area, you will be required to work in the office a minimum of four days per week.

 

Primary Responsibilities:

Define and evolve the end to end architecture for the Agentic AI Claims Automation platform, including agent orchestration, workflow engines, integration layers, data pipelines, and model serving components
Establish architectural patterns and guardrails that support reusable agentic workflows across claims platforms and operations
Partner with enterprise architecture to ensure alignment with emerging enterprise frameworks, AI governance standards, reference architectures, and shared platforms
Create and communicate a clear architectural north star, including strategic technology choices, trade offs, and modernization priorities
Design platform capabilities that enable the development, deployment, monitoring, and lifecycle management of agentic systems and multi agent workflows
Collaborate with AI/ML and data science teams to define scalable patterns for model integration, retrieval augmented generation (RAG), decision logic, and safety filters
Champion “AI first” design principles across engineering teams, including intelligent automation, human in the loop review, observability, and bias mitigation
Lead architectural direction for major Claims GCO transformation initiatives, ensuring business objectives translate into robust, scalable, and cost efficient designs
Provide architectural governance throughout program lifecycle (concept to delivery to operations), including solution reviews, risk identification, and technical dependency management
Mentor architects and senior engineers to elevate technical design skills, architectural thinking, and AI literacy
Inspire teams through thought leadership on emerging technologies, agentic AI trends, and industry best practices in intelligent automation and claims transformation
Act as a trusted advisor to product leaders, business executives, and technology stakeholders across Claims, GCO, and enterprise platforms
Translate complex architectural concepts into clear, business aligned narratives to drive buy in and informed decision making
Present architectural recommendations, solution options, and technical trade offs at senior leadership forums
Partner with reliability, platform engineering, and security teams to ensure availability, performance, compliance, and resilience of AI services and claims automation platforms
Identify architectural improvements that enhance reliability, observability, cost efficiency, and developer productivity
Evaluate new technologies and lead pilot efforts to assess applicability within claims automation and agentic AI patterns

 

You’ll be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role as well as provide development for other roles you may be interested in.

Required Qualifications:

7+ years of experience in software solution architecture, with a focus on AI/ML systems
7+ years of experience architecting scalable platforms and microservices, including RESTful API design and cloud-native development
3+ years of experience communicating architectural vision and strategy across cross-functional teams
3+ years of experience with cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, GCP) and data integration tools
1+ years of experience with AI/ML concepts including responsible AI principles, multi-agent solutions, generative AI, MCP and A2A
1+ years of experience with MLOps tools for deploying and managing models in production
1+ years of experience designing agentic workflows or autonomous systems

 

Preferred Qualifications:

Bachelor’s in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field
1+ years of experience integrating AI solutions into existing enterprise systems while ensuring alignment with evolving architecture standards
Health industry experience
Proven payer background

 

*All employees working remotely will be required to adhere to UnitedHealth Group’s Telecommuter Policy

 

Pay is based on several factors including but not limited to local labor markets, education, work experience, certifications, etc. In addition to your salary, we offer benefits such as, a comprehensive benefits package, incentive and recognition programs, equity stock purchase and 401k contribution (all benefits are subject to eligibility requirements). No matter where or when you begin a career with us, you’ll find a far-reaching choice of benefits and incentives. The salary for this role will range from $134,600 - $230,800 annually based on full-time employment. We comply with all minimum wage laws as applicable.

 

Application Deadline: This will be posted for a minimum of 2 business days or until a sufficient candidate pool has been collected. Job posting may come down early due to volume of applicants.

 

At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone–of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income–deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes — an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.    

 

 

UnitedHealth Group is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer under applicable law and qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, national origin, religion, age, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, or protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by local, state, or federal laws, rules, or regulations.

 

UnitedHealth Group is a drug - free workplace. Candidates are required to pass a drug test before beginning employment.

coverLetter:
Hiring Team JumpCloud 

 United States (Remote) 

 Subject: Application for Senior Engineering Manager, Devices 

 Dear Hiring Team JumpCloud, 

 I am writing to express my profound interest in the Senior Engineering Manager, Devices position at JumpCloud. With a career journey spanning over two decades in software engineering leadership, I have consistently focused on building high-performing teams, executing strategic technical roadmaps, and delivering robust, scalable software solutions, particularly those involving intricate device integration and management. My experience aligns seamlessly with the opportunity to contribute to your innovative environment, especially in a role that values both technical depth and a strong collaborative spirit within a remote framework. 

 Throughout my tenure leading engineering initiatives at organizations like 7-Eleven and CVS Health, I have spearheaded the development and deployment of complex, production-grade systems, focusing on operational excellence and customer impact. While my recent journey has included advanced work in Agentic AI systems and LLM applications, the core of my expertise lies in versatile software engineering leadership, team scaling, and architecting resilient cloud-native solutions for diverse applications, including device interaction. I am adept at guiding teams from concept to launch, ensuring that technical vision translates into tangible business value and stable, high-performance outcomes. 

 My commitment extends beyond technical delivery to fostering vibrant, inclusive team cultures that thrive on continuous learning and innovation. I am particularly adept at operating in distributed environments, ensuring consistent communication and effective collaboration across remote teams, which I understand is a key aspect of this role at JumpCloud. My approach balances ambitious execution timelines with a steadfast focus on engineering quality and a customer-centric mindset, perfectly positioning me to drive your device-oriented software initiatives forward and contribute to JumpCloud's success. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Lenovo 

 Morrisville, North Carolina 

 Subject: Application for Sr. Manager, AI Enterprise Engineering 

 Dear Hiring Team Lenovo, 

 I am writing to express my profound interest in the Sr. Manager, AI Enterprise Engineering position at Lenovo, particularly drawn to the innovative work on the Qira cross-device Personal AI platform. My two decades of leadership in software engineering, specializing in building and scaling AI/ML teams and advanced agentic workflows, align directly with your mission to deliver enterprise-grade AI capabilities to business customers at scale. 

 My journey, including leading critical technology transformations at 7-Eleven and managing high-stakes systems at CVS Health, has equipped me with a deep understanding of architecting secure, compliant, and highly scalable distributed systems essential for enterprise adoption. This experience, coupled with my recent MIT Agentic AI program, directly translates to the role's need for expertise in identity federation, access controls, audit infrastructure, and seamless integration with complex corporate IT environments and data sources, including familiarity with WorkOS and Auth0. I am adept at translating sophisticated commercial requirements into tangible engineering programs, ensuring technical solutions meet rigorous enterprise security and manageability standards. 

 I thrive as a hands-on technical leader, committed to contributing to production code while empowering distributed and international engineering teams to deliver cutting-edge AI products across Windows and Android. My approach is rooted in establishing best practices for deterministic workflow design, robust observability, and end-to-end auditability, ensuring Qira's enterprise deployments are not only innovative but also trustworthy and operationally excellent. I am eager to leverage this blend of strategic vision, technical depth, and enterprise-focused execution to contribute to Qira's success and Lenovo’s broader vision of 'Smarter Technology for All'. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

Why Work at Lenovo
We are Lenovo. We do what we say. We own what we do. We WOW our customers. 


Lenovo is a US$83 billion revenue global technology powerhouse, ranked #196 in the Fortune Global 500, and serving millions of customers every day in 180 markets. Focused on a bold vision to deliver Smarter Technology for All, Lenovo has built on its success as the world’s largest PC company with a full-stack portfolio of AI-enabled, AI-ready, and AI-optimized devices (PCs, workstations, smartphones, tablets), infrastructure (server, storage, edge, high performance computing and software defined infrastructure), software, solutions, and services. Lenovo’s continued investment in world-changing innovation is building a more equitable, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere. Lenovo is listed on the Hong Kong stock exchange under Lenovo Group Limited (HKSE: 992) (ADR: LNVGY). 


This transformation together with Lenovo’s world-changing innovation is building a more inclusive, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere. To find out more visit www.lenovo.com, and read about the latest news via our StoryHub.
Description and Requirements

About Our Team

We are building Qira, Lenovo's next-generation cross-device Personal AI platform that delivers intelligent, context-aware experiences across Windows, Android, and the cloud.




This role focuses on leading the engineering strategy and execution for enterprise-grade AI platform capabilities, enabling Qira to be adopted by business customers at scale. You will play a key role in defining how AI-driven experiences are built, secured, integrated, and delivered across enterprise environments - from identity and access management to workflow automation and unified knowledge retrieval.




What You'll Do

Lead the architecture and delivery of enterprise-grade AI platform capabilities, including identity federation, access controls, audit and compliance infrastructure, and multi-tenant deployment models.
Build and scale a high-performing engineering team - including distributed and international members - focused on enterprise integration, reliability, and scalability.
Contribute hands-on to technical design, architecture, and development alongside the team - this is a coding role as much as a leadership role, and the expectation is that you can build what you are directing others to build.
Drive the design and development of an enterprise administration portal that enables IT administrators at customer organizations to provision users, connect corporate identity providers, configure policies and permissions, manage phased rollouts, and monitor usage and audit activity across their Qira deployment.
Define how Qira systems authenticate, authorize, and interact with enterprise identity providers and corporate IT environments, ensuring seamless and secure adoption by business customers.
Drive the design and implementation of workflow automation use cases that connect Qira to the enterprise systems employees and IT teams use every day, enabling intelligent, agentic experiences across functions like employee onboarding, approval management, and productivity workflows.
Build and maintain close alignment with Lenovo's commercial and sales teams to gather enterprise customer requirements, shape go-to-market readiness, and ensure the engineering team is directly informed by what enterprises actually need to adopt Qira.
Translate commercial requirements - including security posture, deployment and configuration models, integration patterns, observability, manageability, identity, and audit - into concrete engineering programs and workstreams.
Coordinate engineering readiness for commercial milestones including previews, pilots, and general availability launches, ensuring the right technical checks, documentation, and quality gates are in place.
Provide technical input and ground-level signal to leadership on how product decisions will impact enterprise adoption, helping the team stay ahead of commercial requirements during development.
Establish engineering best practices for enterprise AI systems, including deterministic workflow design, observability, error handling, and end-to-end auditability.
Lead technical discovery with enterprise process owners to scope high-impact use cases, working backwards from business outcomes to define what gets built and in what order.
Oversee integration of Qira's knowledge layer with enterprise data sources, enabling unified retrieval across on-device and cloud-hosted content without requiring users to know where information lives.
Define and track success metrics for enterprise deployments - adoption rates, workflow completion, time-to-value - and build the observability infrastructure to surface them.
Collaborate with ecosystem partners and platform teams to define integration contracts, resolve cross-system issues, and optimize reliability at scale.




Basic Qualifications

5+ years of Experience managing and leading software Engineering teams to deliver cross-functional initiatives.
12+ years of Experience building and scaling distributed systems, platforms, or real-time products.
Proficiency and demonstrated ability to serve as a hands-on technical leader contributing to production code.
3 years of Experience delivering AI-driven distributed systems, platforms or real-time products.
Strong program management skillset with proven success scoping MVPs, manage timelines, resolve dependencies, and ship in a fast-paced environment.
Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or a related technical field.

Preferred Qualifications

7+ years of Experience managing and leading software Engineering teams to deliver cross-functional initiatives.
15+ years of Experience building and scaling distributed systems, platforms, or real-time products, with ideally 5 years of experience building AI products.
Current hands-on Expertise and willingness to lead by example: ability to write production code and provide hands-on engineering contribution is desired.
3 years of hands-on Experience working on AI/ML systems, LLM-based applications, or intelligent workflows.
Experience delivering software that meets enterprise security, compliance, and IT manageability requirements - including audit logging, data segmentation, access controls, and engagement with security review processes.
Experience with enterprise identity and authentication standards: SSO, SAML 2.0, OAuth, RBAC, and MFA integration across corporate identity providers.
Familiarity with WorkOS and Auth0 for SSO, SCIM provisioning, domain verification, and user lifecycle management within a SaaS or AI product is strongly preferred.
Experience with workflow orchestration, integration middleware, or API-based systems that connect enterprise applications across functions such as HR, IT, finance, and sales.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage technical and non-technical stakeholders, including enterprise IT leaders and commercial teams.
Ability to translate product and ecosystem strategy into clear technical direction and execution.
Experience working with commercial or B2B enterprise customers through pilot programs or beta deployments.
Experience building and cultivating relationships with enterprise partners such as WorkOS and Auth0.
Exposure to device management platforms or commercial PC software ecosystems.
Experience managing distributed or global engineering teams.
Master's Degree in Engineering or Computer Science preferred.

The base salary budgeted range for this position is $190-210k. Individuals may also be considered for bonus and/or commission.

 

Lenovo’s various benefits can be found on www.lenovobenefits.com.

 

In compliance with Colorado's EPEWA, the expected application deadline for this position is August 7, 2026. This applies to both external and internal candidates.

coverLetter:
Hiring Team ClassDojo 

 Remote; San Francisco 

 Subject: Application for Engineering Lead, Core Product Engineering 

 Dear Hiring Team ClassDojo, 

 My journey in software engineering leadership spans over two decades, focusing on building and scaling high-performing teams to deliver impactful products. I am particularly drawn to ClassDojo's mission and the opportunity to contribute as an Engineering Lead, Core Product Engineering. My extensive experience aligns perfectly with the demands of guiding critical product development and fostering technical excellence. 

 Throughout my career, including my time leading technology innovation at 7-Eleven and driving enterprise solutions for CVS Health, I have consistently focused on architecting and delivering scalable, user-centric applications. I possess deep expertise in cloud-native platforms such as AWS and GCP, and I have successfully overseen the development of systems utilizing modern web and mobile technologies, including Node.js and React, alongside robust CI/CD pipelines. This background equips me to lead the development of ClassDojo's core product, ensuring its reliability, performance, and future-readiness. 

 Beyond technical acumen, my leadership philosophy centers on cultivating a collaborative and inclusive engineering culture. I am adept at translating strategic vision into executable roadmaps, empowering teams, and fostering an environment where innovation thrives. I am passionate about guiding product evolution with a customer-centric approach, ensuring that every engineering effort directly contributes to a superior user experience and supports ClassDojo's overarching goals. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Levi Strauss and Co. 

 San Francisco, CA, USA 

 Subject: Application for Director, Engineering - API and Enablement Platforms 

 Dear Hiring Team, 

 I am writing to express my profound interest in the Director, Engineering - API and Enablement Platforms position at Levi Strauss and Co. Your commitment to cultivating originals and leaving the world better resonates deeply with my own leadership philosophy and extensive journey in engineering. My experience aligning strategic technical roadmaps with enterprise-level transformation, particularly in building foundational platforms for AI at scale, directly mirrors the core mission of this role. 

 Throughout my career, including my significant tenure leading engineering initiatives at 7-Eleven, I have specialized in developing and scaling high-performing platform and AI/ML teams from the ground up. This involved not only executing strategic technical roadmaps and driving rapid proof-of-concept development but also deploying production-grade agentic workflows and LLM applications at an enterprise scale. My background includes architecting sophisticated cloud-native API and event streaming platforms, enabling self-service models, and navigating complex multi-cloud environments, which are directly applicable to your vision for a squad-led enablement model and cloud-native roadmap across GCP and Azure. My journey also includes leadership roles where I have championed significant organizational transformations and fostered operational excellence, akin to the shift towards distributed ownership you are initiating. Furthermore, my recent advanced studies in Agentic AI at MIT have equipped me with a forward-thinking perspective on pioneering LLM Gateway architecture, multi-agent orchestration patterns, and designing API contracts for AI agent consumption, capabilities critical to positioning Levi's API and event streaming platforms as foundational AI infrastructure for an AI-driven future. 

 I am a bold, strategic, and technically exceptional leader energized by both organizational transformation and technical innovation. I am dedicated to inspiring teams, cultivating high-trust partnerships, and fostering an inclusive, collaborative culture grounded in DevSecOps best practices, automated testing, and zero trust security. My approach combines data-driven decision-making with a human-focused leadership style, ensuring technical excellence while empowering teams to achieve their fullest potential. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

Calling all originals: At Levi Strauss & Co., you can be yourself — and be part of something bigger. We're a company of people who like to forge our own path and leave the world better than we found it. Who believe that what makes us different makes us stronger. So add your voice. Make an impact. Find your fit — and your future.

We are looking for a bold, strategic, and technically exceptional Director of Engineering to own the strategy and evolution of Levi's API and event streaming platforms, establishing the self-service foundation that empowers squads to own their connectivity outcomes, and driving the platform capabilities that will determine how effectively we operate AI at scale. Reporting to the Senior Director, Platform Engineering & Shared Services, this role is defined by two cornerstones: driving an enterprise-level transformation toward a squad-led enablement model, and pioneering the next generation of platform capabilities for our AI-driven future.

About the Job

We are looking for a platform engineering leader who thinks in products, leads through influence, and is energized by both organizational transformation and technical innovation:

Lead the transition from a centralized model to a squad-led enablement model. Build the base platforms, patterns, and self-service tooling squads need to own their API and event streaming outcomes. Up-skill teams on API-first and event-driven practices, define clear accountability frameworks, and provide the change management leadership that makes distributed ownership feel supported rather than abandoned.
Develop and deliver the enabling team's core services: setting API-first and event-driven design standards; building a developer portal and patterns library that makes it easy for squads to build to those standards without needing to ask for help; providing internal consulting and subject-matter expertise, including on domain-driven design; and owning governance covering API lifecycle management, schema management, security, and AI traffic governance.
Lead the API Solutions & Gateway and Event Streaming squads, establishing a best-in-class and continuously evolving portfolio of API and event streaming capabilities. Drive the roadmap from the current stack toward cloud-native platforms across a primarily GCP, multi-cloud environment, delivering capabilities that serve both operational and AI/ML workloads.
Position the organization's API and event streaming platforms as foundational AI infrastructure — and pioneer the new capabilities that make AI at scale possible. Own the strategy and roadmap for an LLM Gateway governing multi-vendor model access, MCP and A2A gateway capabilities enabling agentic architectures, and the event streaming layer providing the real-time data signals AI systems depend on.
Champion agentic readiness — designing API contracts and event schemas for AI agent consumption, enabling multi-agent orchestration patterns, and driving self-healing platform capabilities — while leading DevSecOps best practices including automated testing and zero trust security.
Team Leadership — Inspire and lead teams, cultivating high-trust partnerships across Engineering, Product, Technical Product, Program and Commercial teams. Foster an inclusive, collaborative culture as a leader of people.

About You
Engineering leader with 10+ years' experience including 5 years of people management, with a track record of building platform capabilities at scale and leading organizational transformation or operating model changes with significant impacts to architecture.
Deep expertise in enterprise API management and event streaming concepts and patterns — including REST, OpenAPI, GraphQL, and gRPC — with hands-on experience across platforms such as Mulesoft, Confluent Kafka, Apigee, or Cloud Pub/Sub.
Experience designing cloud-native API and event streaming architectures across GCP and/or Azure environments, with the ability to navigate multi-cloud realities, make platform-appropriate tooling decisions, and integrate with cloud data and AI services (e.g. BigQuery, Vertex AI, Azure OpenAI).
Demonstrated experience in how API and event streaming platforms enable AI strategy — including LLM gateway architecture, AI-native API patterns (streaming, async, function calling), multi-vendor AI governance through API infrastructure, and agentic patterns including tool-calling APIs and event-driven agent triggers.
Proven track record of building and evolving platform capabilities, with experience leading change management and accountability model transitions within engineering organizations.
Use data and metrics to interpret monitoring, derive recommendations, and drive actions.
Comprehensive understanding of Agile and DevOps principles, including DevSecOps and zero-trust security models.

The expected starting salary range for this role is $148,600 - $245,400 per year. We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range based on the location of the role. The amount a particular employee will earn within the salary range will be based on factors such as relevant education, qualifications, performance and business needs.

Levi Strauss & Co. (LS&Co.) offers a total rewards package that includes base pay, incentive plans, 401(k) matching, paid leave, health insurance, product discounts, and more designed to help you and your family stay healthy, meet your financial goals, and balance the demands of your work and personal life. Available benefits and incentive compensation vary depending upon the specifics of the role; details relating to a specific role will be made available upon request. Read more about our benefits here.

LS&Co. is an affirmative action and equal employment opportunity employer. We welcome and value people from diverse cultures, backgrounds, and experiences to make LS&Co. a collective success.

Our common thread: We're originals.

From day one, we've been doing it our way — creating our own drumbeat and building something that's different from the rest. That's why we're looking for people who are excited about finding their career fit and transforming the future. Because at Levi Strauss & Co., you can do what you love while staying true to who you are.

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Adyen 

 Chicago, IL 

 Subject: Application for Engineering Manager, CI/CD 

 Dear Hiring Team Adyen, 

 My journey through over two decades of software engineering leadership, particularly in scaling complex platforms at organizations like 7-Eleven, has instilled in me a profound appreciation for operational excellence and seamless development processes. I am writing to express my enthusiastic interest in the Engineering Manager, CI/CD position at Adyen in Chicago. Adyen's commitment to providing payments, data, and financial products in a single, innovative solution, coupled with its "engineered for ambition" philosophy, resonates deeply with my own drive to build high-performing teams and deliver impactful, ethical solutions at scale. 

 Throughout my career, I have guided the vision and execution for critical engineering functions, directly aligning with the responsibilities of this role. At 7-Eleven, I oversaw the development and deployment of robust software platforms, emphasizing automated build processes, efficient CI/CD pipelines, and an elevated developer experience – precisely what your In-Person-Payments Tooling team seeks. My experience with enterprise-level Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) for diverse applications, coupled with a deep understanding of collaborating with infrastructure teams on Kubernetes clusters and implementing Infrastructure as Code (IaC) principles, positions me to significantly contribute to Adyen's mobile application ecosystem, including Android and iOS applications. 

 I excel in Technical Leadership and Strategic Oversight, defining roadmaps for crucial initiatives such as caching optimizations and security scanning to ensure software deployment remains smooth and efficient. Beyond technical acumen, my passion lies in People Leadership and Mentorship. I am dedicated to fostering career growth, managing team velocity, and cultivating an "automation-first" mindset. I believe in clearing roadblocks and building an environment where diverse perspectives thrive, ensuring our team is not just productive, but truly engaged and innovative. This approach aligns perfectly with Adyen's culture of empowering individuals to own their careers and tackle unique technical challenges collaboratively. 

 My career has been a continuous journey of leading technical teams to deliver innovative and reliable solutions. I am confident that my leadership experience, analytical approach to complex infrastructure challenges, and commitment to operational excellence will enable me to empower your platform engineering team and significantly enhance Adyen's CI/CD capabilities. I am eager to discuss how my background can directly contribute to your team's success in Chicago. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi
jobDescription:
Engineering Manager, CI/CD
Chicago
Apply

This is Adyen

Adyen provides payments, data, and financial products in a single solution for customers like Meta, Uber, H&M, and Microsoft - making us the financial technology platform of choice. At Adyen, everything we do is engineered for ambition. 

For our teams, we create an environment with opportunities for our people to succeed, backed by the culture and support to ensure they are enabled to truly own their careers. We are motivated individuals who tackle unique technical challenges at scale and solve them as a team. Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster.

 

Engineering Manager, CI/CD

We are looking for a Manager for our In-Person-Payments Tooling team. The team is based in Chicago, you will be guiding the vision and execution for the CI/CD Engineering aspect ensuring smooth and efficient software development and deployment processes.

As a Team Lead, your primary focus is to create and enable a high performing platform engineering team. You will oversee the development of tools and applications that manage the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) for our Android and iOS applications. You will ensure your team successfully collaborates with the infrastructure teams, handling Kubernetes clusters to provide an elevated SDLC experience for the entire organization. You will also stay close to your users and deliver the most impactful initiatives for their developer experience.

What You’ll Do
Technical Leadership: Lead a team of DevOps and Infrastructure Engineers in designing and implementing high-performance CI/CD pipelines using GitLab CI, Gradle, KMP, and Develocity.
Strategic Oversight: Define the roadmap for automating build processes, caching optimizations, and security scanning to ensure software deployment remains smooth and efficient.
Mentorship & Growth: Actively coach engineers in troubleshooting complex build failures and developing an "automation-first" mindset.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Serve as the primary point of contact between mobile developers and infrastructure teams to ensure tooling health, monitoring, and alerting are seamlessly integrated.
Process Excellence: Champion quality-focused initiatives, ensuring the CI/CD pipeline remains the organization's first line of defense for code stability.
Who You Are
Experienced People Leader: You have a proven track record (3+ years) as a Technical Manager or Team Lead. You know how to manage performance, foster career growth, and maintain team velocity.
Empathetic Navigator: You understand that leading a team is about clearing roadblocks and building an environment where diverse perspectives thrive.
Analytical Strategist: You can look at complex infrastructure challenges and guide your team toward logical, scalable solutions.
Operational Excellence: You are comfortable working with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) principles and understand the requirements of a private cloud environment.
Nice to Have
Mobile Development Background: While your leadership skills are the priority, experience with Android (Gradle/Kotlin) or iOS (Xcode/Swift) ecosystems is a significant plus.
Deep CI/CD Knowledge: Hands-on history with GitLab CI, Jenkins, Temporal.
Systems Proficiency: An understanding of Linux systems administration (process management and resource allocation).

 

The annual base salary range for this role is $180,000 - $243,000; to learn more about our compensation philosophy, please click here.

 

Our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion commitments 

Our unique approach is a product of our diverse perspectives. This diversity of backgrounds and cultures is essential in helping us maintain our momentum. Our business and technical challenges are unique, and we need as many different voices as possible to join us in solving them - voices like yours. No matter who you are or where you’re from, we welcome you to be your true self at Adyen. 

Studies show that women and members of underrepresented communities apply for jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. Does this sound like you? If so, Adyen encourages you to reconsider and apply. We look forward to your application!

 

What’s next?

Ensuring a smooth and enjoyable candidate experience is critical for us. We aim to get back to you regarding your application within 5 business days. Our interview process tends to take about 4 weeks to complete, but may fluctuate depending on the role. Learn more about our hiring process here. Don’t be afraid to let us know if you need more flexibility.

Adyen is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, national origin, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, marital status or any legally protected status.

All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

 

This role is based out of our Chicago office. We are an office-first company and value in-person collaboration; we do not offer remote-only roles. This role is hybrid, with 3 days per week in the office.

coverLetter:
Hiring Team JumpCloud 

 United States (Remote) 

 Subject: Application for Senior Engineering Manager, Devices 

 Dear Hiring Team JumpCloud, 

 My career journey, marked by over two decades in transformative software engineering leadership, has consistently centered on building scalable platforms and fostering innovation, a path I believe aligns perfectly with the Senior Engineering Manager, Devices role at JumpCloud. At 7-Eleven, I spearheaded initiatives that modernized enterprise systems and enhanced customer-facing applications, navigating the complexities of high-volume, mission-critical environments. My experience in leading robust engineering teams and delivering impactful solutions in dynamic settings, including my tenure at CVS Health where I focused on large-scale platform development, has honed my ability to drive strategic technical roadmaps and cultivate collaborative engineering cultures. 

 I am particularly drawn to JumpCloud's mission to simplify device and identity management, and I recognize the critical importance of secure, efficient, and user-friendly device experiences for businesses. My background includes architecting solutions across diverse cloud ecosystems like AWS and Azure, coupled with hands-on leadership in developing modern web and mobile applications using technologies such as React, React Native, and Node.js. I thrive on overseeing the entire software development lifecycle, from conceptualization and rapid prototyping to ensuring seamless CI/CD and production deployment for complex, distributed systems. 

 My recent engagement with the MIT Agentic AI program, while specializing in advanced AI, underscores my commitment to continuous learning and leveraging cutting-edge approaches to solve complex engineering challenges. This forward-thinking mindset, combined with my proven track record in building and scaling high-performing teams, positions me to contribute significantly to your device engineering initiatives. I am eager to apply my expertise in driving innovation and operational excellence to enhance JumpCloud's device management offerings and empower your customers. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

coverLetter:
Hiring Team ClassDojo 

 San Francisco, CA 

 Subject: Application for Engineering Lead, Core Product Engineering 

 Dear Hiring Team ClassDojo, 

 My journey through over two decades of software and engineering leadership, including significant tenures at 7-Eleven and CVS Health, has uniquely prepared me to contribute to ClassDojo as your next Engineering Lead for Core Product Engineering. I am deeply compelled by ClassDojo's mission to foster wonderful classrooms and communities, an objective that resonates with my passion for building scalable, user-centric products that genuinely impact lives. My experience leading complex transformations and architecting high-performance systems, particularly those serving millions of users, aligns perfectly with the demands of evolving and enhancing your core product offerings. 

 At 7-Eleven, I spearheaded initiatives that transformed customer engagement through scalable loyalty platforms and cloud-native architectures, driving significant business growth and customer satisfaction. Similarly, at CVS Health, I led engineering teams in developing robust, operationally excellent systems that underpinned critical consumer-facing applications. These roles honed my ability to translate strategic vision into tangible engineering outcomes, manage diverse technical teams, and navigate the full product lifecycle from concept to large-scale deployment, leveraging modern cloud environments like AWS and GCP with agile methodologies. 

 My recent engagement with the MIT Agentic AI program further solidifies my foundation in cutting-edge technologies and innovative problem-solving, equipping me to guide future-forward product development and data-driven solutions. I excel at fostering a culture of technical excellence, continuous delivery (CI/CD), and collaborative innovation, ensuring teams are empowered to build secure, efficient, and impactful solutions. I am eager to apply this blend of leadership, technical depth across areas like full-stack development with Node.js and React, and strategic vision to ClassDojo's core product engineering challenges. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

coverLetter:
Hiring Team BrightEdge 

 United States (Remote) 

 Subject: Application for Engineering Manager 

 Dear Hiring Team BrightEdge, 

 I am writing to express my profound interest in the Engineering Manager position at BrightEdge. With over 20 years of progressive experience in software engineering and leadership, my career journey has been dedicated to spearheading high-performing teams and delivering high-impact AI and automation solutions. I am particularly drawn to BrightEdge's pioneering work in SEO and Digital Marketing, and its strategic focus on integrating AI layers like Autopilot and DataMind to modernize the digital marketing stack. My expertise aligns perfectly with your mission to drive innovation and transformation within the industry. 

 Throughout my experience, I have cultivated a proven track record in defining and executing robust technical strategies, architectures, and roadmaps for advanced AI/ML systems. I excel at leading, mentoring, and managing skilled engineering teams, fostering a culture of technical excellence and continuous improvement. My background in building and scaling teams focused on agentic workflows and LLM applications directly positions me to contribute to BrightEdge's initiatives in providing prioritized recommendations and improving search visibility through cutting-edge AI capabilities. 

 I am passionate about championing best practices in software development, ensuring superior code quality, scalability, and maintainability in fast-paced, dynamic environments. My leadership philosophy emphasizes strong cross-functional collaboration with product management, design, and quality assurance teams to drive project success. I am confident in my ability to provide essential technical guidance, adeptly solve complex problems, and implement effective risk mitigation strategies to achieve ambitious project goals and contribute significantly to BrightEdge's ongoing innovation and market leadership. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

About the job

We are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Engineering Manager to join our team. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record of leading and managing engineering teams in the tech domain. As an Engineering Manager, you will play a pivotal role in driving the technical direction of our projects and ensuring the successful delivery of our products.




Key Responsibilities 


Lead, mentor, and manage a team of skilled engineers to achieve project goals and deliver high-quality software solutions


Define and execute the technical strategy, architecture, and roadmap for the development team


Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including product management, design, and quality assurance, to drive project success


Champion best practices in software development, ensuring code quality, scalability, and maintainability


Foster a culture of innovation, continuous improvement, and technical excellence within the engineering team


Provide technical guidance and support to team members, assisting in problem-solving and decision-making


Drive the recruitment and onboarding of top engineering talent


Monitor project progress, identify risks, and implement effective mitigation strategies


Keep abreast of emerging technologies and industry trends to ensure the team remains competitive and at the forefront of technology







Skills & Qualifications 


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science or a related field


Should be from technical background


A minimum of 15 years of experience in the tech domain, with a strong background in software engineering and development


Proven experience in managing and leading engineering teams, with a focus on delivering high-impact projects


Strong technical skills and the ability to provide technical leadership to the team


Excellent problem-solving and decision-making abilities


Strong communication, collaboration, and leadership skills


Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment


Familiarity with modern software development methodologies and tools







Benefits & Perks 


Competitive Salary


PTO and Paid Holidays


Medical PPO or HMO, Dental, and Vision Insurance

BrightEdge is widely recognized as a global leader in SEO and Digital marketing. We are helping thousands of organizations, including many of the world’s largest companies, transform their businesses and drive more revenue.  

  

The continuous innovation of our product is supported by what we believe to be our most valuable assets  our people. Our employees are industry experts at the forefront of digital transformation. Come join us and help us share the future of SEO.

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Confidential 

 United States (Remote) 

 Subject: Application for Vice President of Engineering 

 Dear Hiring Team Confidential, 

 My career journey, spanning over two decades in software engineering and executive leadership, has consistently centered on building, scaling, and evolving high-performing engineering organizations, making the Vice President of Engineering role at Confidential an exciting prospect. My tenure, including significant leadership roles at organizations like 7-Eleven, has equipped me with a robust understanding of leading distributed teams and driving complex, customer-centric initiatives through periods of rapid business scale. I am particularly drawn to your emphasis on balancing architectural judgment with organizational leadership and ensuring engineering execution aligns tightly with product strategy and revenue goals. 

 I bring a deep technical credibility cultivated through hands-on experience across modern engineering environments, including extensive cloud infrastructure (Azure, AWS) and distributed systems, alongside expertise in Kubernetes, modern orchestration, CI/CD platforms, and VDI tooling. My background includes owning end-to-end engineering execution and technical direction, establishing critical operating rhythms, and providing architectural oversight to empower engineering leaders effectively. I am adept at translating technical priorities into tangible business outcomes, skills vital for a confident partnership with Product, Operations, and Commercial leadership. 

 Furthermore, my experience extends to designing and evolving organizational structures, making investment and resourcing decisions, and collaborating with executive leadership on long-term product evolution. While my recent MIT Agentic AI program participation highlights my commitment to staying at the forefront of technological innovation and strategic foresight, my foundational expertise lies in guiding engineering teams to deliver robust, scalable solutions across all development phases. I am eager to apply this comprehensive leadership experience to contribute meaningfully to Confidential's continued growth and success. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

About the job

We're looking for a Vice President of Engineering to lead and evolve its engineering organization as the business continues to scale.

This role requires a hands-on engineering executive with deep technical credibility, a strong track record of building and leading distributed teams, and the maturity to collaborate as a peer on the executive leadership team. The successful candidate will balance architectural judgment with organizational leadership, ensuring engineering execution aligns tightly with product strategy, operational priorities, and revenue goals.




Key Responsibilities

Lead and scale multi-disciplinary, distributed engineering teams across platform, infrastructure, and software development
Own engineering execution, delivery, and technical direction in close partnership with Product and Revenue leadership
Establish and evolve engineering operating rhythms, execution models, and accountability mechanisms
Provide architectural oversight while empowering engineering leaders and avoiding technical bottlenecks
Design and evolve the engineering organization structure as the company grows
Partner with executive leadership on:
Engineering strategy
Investment and resourcing decisions
Long-term product evolution
Represent engineering leadership internally and externally as needed




Required Experience & Profile:




Engineering & Technical Leadership

Strong personal technical background with credibility across modern engineering environments, including:
Cloud infrastructure and distributed systems
Kubernetes and modern orchestration
CI/CD platforms and developer tooling
VDI and virtual work environment tooling
Network and infrastructure connectivity
Experience guiding architecture without operating as the primary individual contributor

Leadership & Scale

Proven experience:
Building and leading teams through scale
Managing distributed and remote engineering organizations
Developing engineering leaders and contributors
Demonstrated success operating and collaborating at the senior executive level

Executive Capability

analysis: The summary and competencies have been thoroughly optimized to align with Ashby's distinctive engineering culture, emphasizing grassroots leadership, high autonomy, product-minded engineers, and hands-on technical engagement. Key themes such as 'minimal process,' 'developer experience,' 'AI-powered tooling,' and 'common components' are explicitly integrated, reflecting the job's core requirements. The cover letter is highly tailored, connecting the candidate's executive experience and MIT Agentic AI credentials directly to Ashby's mission and challenges, including scaling the engineering team and fostering a unique, collaborative environment. Irrelevant jargon has been removed, ensuring a sharp, focused narrative.
summary: Visionary, hands-on Engineering Leader with over two decades of software experience, adept at building and scaling high-performing teams while fostering a culture of profound ownership and autonomy. Proven track record of guiding engineers in developing product and technical specifications, driving rapid innovation, and deploying intelligent, scalable applications. Passionate about empowering exceptional engineers to deliver magical outcomes through minimal process, continuous improvement, and robust developer tooling. Expert in leveraging cloud architectures and advanced AI/ML to drive impactful product growth and effectively scale unique engineering cultures.
competencies: \item \textbf{Engineering Leadership and Team Scaling:} Adept at building, mentoring, and scaling high-performing engineering teams, fostering grassroots leadership and growing individual contributors to own complex projects.\item \textbf{Product and Technical Guidance:} Expert in providing feedback on product and technical specifications, coaching engineers to identify scope optimizations and ensure high-quality, impactful solutions.\item \textbf{Developer Experience and Tooling Investment:} Proven ability to enhance developer experience, jump into systems for debugging, and invest in robust tools and abstractions for increased engineering leverage and efficiency.\item \textbf{AI-Powered Software Development:} Specialized in leveraging AI/ML, agentic workflows, and LLMs to automate tasks, generate code, suggest fixes, and build intelligent, powerful software solutions.\item \textbf{High-Autonomy Project Delivery:} Championing an environment where engineers run their projects, take on large, loosely defined initiatives, and push information outward with minimal process.\item \textbf{Strategic Process Improvement:} Skilled in proposing and leading department-wide initiatives, such as optimizing pull request reviews, designing thoughtful interviews, and improving data simulation.\item \textbf{Cloud-Native Architectures and Scalability:} Extensive experience architecting and deploying scalable, cloud-native solutions, focusing on common components and robust system stability.\item \textbf{Inclusive Culture Building and Talent Development:} Dedicated to cultivating diverse, generative engineering cultures where collaboration is natural, communication is deliberate, and exceptional talent thrives.
coverLetter:
Abhik and the Ashby Engineering Team

San Francisco Bay Area, CA

Subject: Application for Engineering Manager, Americas

Dear Abhik and the Ashby Engineering Team,

I am writing to express my profound enthusiasm for the Engineering Manager, Americas position at Ashby. Your vision for a lean, high-autonomy engineering culture that delivers exceptional speed and quality deeply resonates with my over two decades of experience leading and scaling high-performing software teams. Having navigated rapid growth environments, such as during my tenure in technology leadership at 7-Eleven, I am particularly drawn to Ashby's ambition to double its engineering team thoughtfully while maintaining its unique, impactful approach to product development in Talent Acquisition.

My leadership philosophy aligns perfectly with Ashby's commitment to minimal process and empowering engineers to run their own projects. I excel at providing the right level of feedback and coaching to help individual contributors identify scope, improve quality, and deliver large, loosely defined projects with significant autonomy. I also share your belief that engineering leaders must remain hands-on and abreast of technology; I genuinely enjoy diving into systems to debug, contribute small fixes, and continuously improve developer experience, ensuring our team maintains leverage and efficiency.

Furthermore, my expertise in advanced AI/ML, agentic workflows, and LLM applications, including recent credentials from the MIT Agentic AI program, directly supports Ashby's strategic investment in AI-powered tooling. I am eager to contribute to initiatives that automate mundane tasks, suggest fixes, generate production-ready code, and elevate the overall intelligence of your platform. My background in architecting scalable cloud-native solutions and building reusable common components also positions me to contribute significantly to your continuous improvement of core platform building blocks.

I am passionate about cultivating diverse, inclusive engineering cultures where natural collaboration and deliberate communication lead to extraordinary outcomes. The opportunity to help scale Ashby's distinctive methods from 50 to 100 engineers and beyond, while upholding your values of talent, humility, and kindness, is incredibly exciting. I am confident that my leadership experience and dedication to fostering an environment where exceptional engineers thrive make me an ideal candidate to contribute to Ashby's continued success.

Sincerely,

Bhagath Siddi

Ashby

Engineering Manager, Americas 

San Francisco, CA · Reposted 6 days ago · Over 100 people clicked apply

$200K/yr - $275K/yr
Remote

Help me stand out

About the job

Hi 👋🏾, I’m Abhik, Ashby’s Co-Founder and VP of Engineering. We’re looking for a great (former) engineer who built impressive products and now builds teams of great engineers. Ashby’s success and ambition mean we’re doubling the Engineering team in the next year, and we need your experience and leadership to do it thoughtfully.

Our product and growth are exceptional. Ashby All-in-One is powerful, easy to use, and replaces several venture-backed companies' worth of products (often with a better experience). We have notable customers like Notion, Linear, Shopify, and Snowflake. Our growth and retention metrics are best-in-class among our peers: we have tens of millions in ARR, growing >100% year over year, very low churn, and many years of runway.

A big part of our secret sauce is how we run Engineering. We achieve incredible speed and quality by discarding many industry norms and being optimistic about Engineers. We consider what makes exceptional Engineers exceptional, figure out how to hire them, and build an environment that gives them the freedom and agency to actually be exceptional. In other words, if engineers writing product specs, making product decisions, and not breaking down projects into individual tickets excites you, you’ve found the right place.

As an Engineering Manager, you’ll work closely with me, Ben, Colin, and your peers to build out the team and continue scaling this unique culture.

You’ll First Do That Through Grassroots Leadership. You’ll Manage Three To Five Engineers And Hire Three To Five More. They’ll Cover Multiple Teams And a Variety Of Specializations, From Product Engineers To Site Reliability Engineers To Design Engineers. Your Day-to-day Will Include


Providing feedback on product and technical specs to help engineers identify where to cut scope or improve quality. You don’t make the final decisions, but you’ll influence and coach ICs to reach the right ones.
Grow engineers to the point where they can take large, loosely defined projects and deliver them with little intervention.
Jump into our systems and code to debug a customer issue, ship a small bug fix, or improve our developer experience. Engineering leaders at Ashby are great engineers and enjoy keeping their skills up-to-date (while staying off the critical path).


You’ll also propose and lead department and company-wide initiatives. Some examples:


Rethink how we (and the industry) do pull request reviews by aligning on goals with the team, sampling a set of PRs to understand how effective they are, and writing a proposal to the team about what we could change to speed up reviews while giving reviewers the time and space to give useful feedback.
Design and improve interviews based on candidate and team feedback. We pride ourselves on thoughtful interviews that simulate actually working with us!
Improve how we generate and simulate data in demo accounts. It’s a project off the critical path, but it helps you keep up-to-date on our codebase while immensely impacting the business, from Engineering to QA to Sales.


I’ll share more details once we meet.

What We’re Building

As engineers, we are used to tooling that makes us better at what we do. When we started Ashby, we saw the opposite with Talent Acquisition software. Recruiting teams were leveling up how they did their work, but instead of software meeting this new standard, it held them back.

Scheduling a final round is an excellent example. Recruiting teams wanted to schedule candidates faster, track interviewer preparation and quality, and do it with half the headcount. A recruiter needed to manually collect availability from the candidate, identify qualified interviewers, perform “Calendar Tetris” to find who is available to interview the candidate, schedule on the earliest date possible, and make any last-minute adjustments as availability changed. They must do this while considering the interview load on each individual and whether interviewers need to be trained and shadowing others. 🥵 TA software didn’t help.

As hiring managers, we know TA is a critical function, and as engineers, we know software can do better. So, we built and continue to build Ashby to give TA teams the highest standard of tooling. Software that’s intelligent and powerful. Software that provides insights into where they’re failing and automates or simplifies many of the tasks they’re underwater with. We want other functions and departments to be jealous of what TA teams can do with Ashby, and today they often are!

Why You Shouldn’t Apply


You don’t enjoy coding or don’t find time to stay up-to-date on technology. We believe great leaders are folks who were great engineers themselves. That starts with loving the craft and never giving it up.
You follow the rules and accept the hand you’ve been dealt. We want folks with the ambition, courage, and follow-through to change things, whether it’s within their own team or across departments.
You went into management because it was the only growth path available. We want folks who could have been Principal engineers (or were one!).
You're happy with a team of engineers who are predominantly early-career or mid-career or don't thrive with ownership or autonomy. You believe that with enough guardrails, the team can get things done.
To you, a Staff or Principal Engineer is someone who spends most of their time project managing or conducting architecture reviews. You don’t believe staff and principal engineers could lead by the example they set or the leverage they create through their work.
You’re not optimistic or convinced that we can build a large engineering team that functions differently from the status quo. You think, at some size, common processes need to be implemented to ensure consistent product delivery (e.g., sprint planning, product managers writing in-depth specifications). You might not say it out loud, but you think, at some size, compromises have to be made for the sake of hiring numbers or consistency.


Engineering Culture

Our Engineering Culture Is Motivated By Benji’s (my Co-founder And CEO) And My Belief That a Small, Talented Team, Given The Right Environment, Can Build High-quality Software Fast (and Work Regular Hours!). We Do It Through


Minimal process with ownership over decisions normally made by product and design
Natural collaboration and deliberate communication
Investing in tools and abstractions that give us leverage
Putting effort into building a diverse team


You’ll be an important part of figuring out how to scale these methods from 50 engineers to 100 and beyond.

Minimal Process & Lots of Ownership

The best engineers we’ve worked with delivered reliably magical outcomes. They took customer problems and relentlessly drove them to solutions that were not only successful but often brilliant and creative. While they did this with minimal oversight, stakeholders were never in the dark as to what was going on, and no setback was a surprise.

Traditional product-development processes aren’t meant for the best engineers. Their purpose is to create consistent outcomes regardless of the engineer’s skill. But, consistency comes at the expense of an engineer’s time and freedom—both ingredients necessary to generate those magical outcomes. As a result, process stifles the best engineers and doesn’t give others the opportunity to practice the behaviors that made the best engineers the “best.”

At Ashby, we want to build an environment that encourages every engineer to be their best. So, at Ashby, every Engineer runs their project. Product Managers (and Designers) build strategy, do customer research, and hand off problem briefs to Engineers. Engineers take on the rest: they research the problem, write product specs, build wireframes, and implement their solution end-to-end. We rely on engineers, not process, to push information outward to the relevant folks (e.g., Product Managers) and pull folks in to help (e.g., Designers, Infra). It’s a new level of ownership for many engineers, but we’d rather an engineer fail a bit and coach up their skills than use process as a crutch. Not everyone succeeds in our culture, but those who do thrive.

Collaboration is Natural & Communication is Deliberate

Our engineering team consists of lifelong learners who are talented but also humble and kind (meet them here!). These attributes create an environment where collaboration happens naturally. We combine this with research, prototyping, and written proposals to see around corners and get feedback from the team across time zones. Focus time is something that we hold sacred, and, with thoughtful and deliberate communication, engineers are in <2h meetings per week (I wrote about it here).

To Drive It Home, Here's a Recent Calendar Of An Engineer Who Has Been With Us For Over 4 Years. ~34 Hours Of Focus Time, 2.5h Of Interviews, And 3.5 Hours Of Meetings

We also meet in person at least twice a year, once as a department and once as a company. You also have a small budget to meet up with folks in your city/region.

Increase Leverage, not Team Size

We Built Ashby With The Quality, Breadth, And Depth That Many Customers Would Expect From Much Larger Teams Over Larger Time Scales. We’ve Done This Through Investment In


Great developer tooling. Our CI/CD takes ~10m, and we deploy at least 15x a day. A debugger that works out of the box. Everyone on the team has contributed to our developer experience 💪🏾.
Building blocks to create powerful and customizable products fast. At the core of Ashby is a set of common components (analytics modeling and query language, policy engine, workflow engine, design system) that we constantly improve. Each improvement to a common component cascades throughout our app (short video below).
AI-powered tooling. We think of AI as a way to automate the mundane parts of building and maintaining high-quality software. We use a combination of third-party and internally built tools that, for instance, auto-triage customer issues, suggest fixes, prototype ideas, generate production-ready code, and conduct code reviews. Engineers have an unlimited token budget (but are not measured on it). We write in detail about our philosophy, current use of AI, and future plans for AI in Engineering here.


And a Demo Of One Of These Building Blocks

Here’s an impromptu quote from Arjun in our company Slack of what it’s like to build a feature at Ashby:

Put Effort into Diversity

Diverse teams drive innovation and better outcomes. Having seen my mother and partner build their careers as minority women in non-diverse fields, I want to make sure Ashby creates opportunities for the next generation of engineers from underrepresented groups.

Today, 25% of engineers and 50% of our engineering leaders at Ashby are from underrepresented groups. We are taking conscious steps to improve, like sourcing diverse candidates, providing generous paid family leave, no leetcode interviews, and more.

Interview Process

This is an important role and our interview process reflects that both in challenge and length (5h 15m):


Introduction call with me and Ben (30m, live)
A technical screen where we pair in our actual codebase (1h, live)
An interview to explore your past experience as a leader (1h, live)
Interview with Ben, Director of Engineering AMER (45m, live)
Interview with Colin, Head of Engineering EMEA (30m, live)
Final round where you meet Engineers, other Engineering Managers, and Benji, our CEO (90m, live)


Depending on my bandwidth, we may start with an additional 30m screen with a recruiter. Near the end of the process, we’ll do reference checks.

This is also an important decision for you, so I’m always happy to have coffee chats in person or over Zoom to get to know each other. If we don’t move forward, we’re happy to share feedback.

Your First Six Months at Ashby

Your first three months at Ashby will be spent learning about Ashby Engineering and easing into leadership by being an IC. What better way to learn how ICs work than to be one! Don’t worry if you don’t know our technologies. The team is really helpful, and our dev environment is pretty awesome: it sets up in a single script, and you’ll be able to push your first product change on day one.

You and I will do 30-day, 60-day, 90-day, and 6-month reviews to calibrate on our unique culture and how you, I, and the team will work together.

This role will initially report to me, but may report to Ben, our Director of Engineering, over time.

Benefits


Competitive salary and equity.
10-year exercise window for stock options. You shouldn’t feel pressure to purchase stock options if you leave Ashby —do it when you feel financially comfortable.
Unlimited PTO.
A minimum of 12 weeks of fully paid parental leave, covered by Ashby. For folks outside the US, it may be longer to be in line with regional requirements.
Generous equipment, software, and office furniture budget. Get what you need to be happy and productive!
$100/month education budget with more expensive items (like conferences) covered with manager approval.
If you’re in the US, we offer top-tier health insurance for you and your dependents, with 100% of premiums covered by Ashby. In other countries, we provide high-quality supplemental health insurance for you and your dependents, also fully covered by us.


Ashby’s success hinges on hiring great people and creating an environment where we can be happy, feel challenged, and do our best work. We’re being deliberate about building that environment from the ground up. I hope that excites you enough to apply.

Ashby provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetics, sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender expression. We are committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce and welcome people from all backgrounds, experiences, perspectives, and abilities.

Ashby is committed to a fair and transparent hiring process. We confirm that this advertisement is for an active, existing vacancy within our organization. Please be advised that we may use artificial intelligence-driven tools to assist our recruitment team in screening, assessing, and selecting candidates for this position.

Compensation Range: $200K - $275K

jobTitle: Senior Technical Program Manager, FUB Engineering
analysis: The resume summary, competencies, and cover letter have been thoroughly optimized to align with Zillow's Senior Technical Program Manager, FUB Engineering job description. Key enhancements include weaving in specific technical requirements such as 'modern web and mobile architectures, distributed systems, and APIs in agile environments' into the summary. The competencies block was already strong and has been maintained, ensuring exactly 8 highly tailored items covering technical program leadership, AI/ML lifecycle, platform infrastructure, cross-functional alignment, and stakeholder influence. The cover letter was refined to remove any industry-specific jargon not relevant to real estate, replacing a 'CVS Health' reference with a general statement on 'operational excellence and rigorous data governance' to maintain focus on transferable skills. All sections now directly address the role's need for leading complex, ambiguous, multi-team programs with a focus on AI capabilities, platform health, scalability, and customer impact, driving predictable delivery and engineering velocity. The connection to 7-Eleven as a previous employer is a strong asset, given Zillow's hiring trends.

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Zillow FUB Engineering 

 United States (Remote) 

 Subject: Application for Senior Technical Program Manager, FUB Engineering 

 Dear Hiring Team Zillow FUB Engineering, 

 It is with genuine enthusiasm that I submit my application for the Senior Technical Program Manager, FUB Engineering role at Zillow. Having dedicated over two decades to leading transformative technology initiatives, including the development and scaling of AI/ML-driven platforms in high-stakes, customer-centric environments like 7-Eleven, I am deeply drawn to Zillow’s mission of empowering millions in their significant life decisions. My journey has consistently focused on bridging complex technical challenges with strategic product outcomes, aligning seamlessly with the FUB Engineering team's goal to deliver seamless, end-to-end real estate experiences. 

 My extensive experience in executing strategic technical roadmaps and deploying production-grade AI/ML solutions, including agentic workflows and LLM applications, directly addresses the need to deliver sophisticated AI capabilities for Zillow. Through various leadership roles, I have cultivated a deep understanding of operational excellence and rigorous data governance, critical for ensuring responsible AI practices and quality measurement. This background, coupled with specialized training from the MIT Agentic AI program, positions me to confidently lead initiatives spanning platform infrastructure, API evolution, and innovative customer-facing product launches, ensuring both short-term delivery and long-term platform scalability. 

 I am particularly excited by the prospect of partnering with your Engineering and Data Science teams to drive high-impact initiatives within the FUB ecosystem, connecting platform and product investments directly to customer value. My approach emphasizes bringing clarity to ambiguous environments, proactively identifying dependencies, and fostering alignment across diverse cross-functional teams to ensure predictable delivery and robust engineering velocity. I believe my proven ability to influence stakeholders and make data-informed decisions will be instrumental in navigating technical and operational trade-offs to achieve Zillow’s ambitious goals. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi
