Why are you being asked to complete this form?

We are a federal contractor or subcontractor. The law requires us to provide equal employment opportunity to qualified people with disabilities. We have a goal of having at least 7% of our workers as people with disabilities. The law says we must measure our progress towards this goal. To do this, we must ask applicants and employees if they have a disability or have ever had one. People can become disabled, so we need to ask this question at least every five years.

Completing this form is voluntary, and we hope that you will choose to do so. Your answer is confidential. No one who makes hiring decisions will see it. Your decision to complete the form and your answer will not harm you in any way. If you want to learn more about the law or this form, visit the U.S. Department of Labor’s Office of Federal Contract Compliance Programs (OFCCP) website at www.dol.gov/ofccp.

How do you know if you have a disability?

A disability is a condition that substantially limits one or more of your “major life activities.” If you have or have ever had such a condition, you are a person with a disability. Disabilities include, but are not limited to:

Alcohol or other substance use disorder (not currently using drugs illegally)
Autoimmune disorder, for example, lupus, fibromyalgia, rheumatoid arthritis, HIV/AIDS
Blind or low vision
Cancer (past or present)
Cardiovascular or heart disease
Celiac disease
Cerebral palsy
Deaf or serious difficulty hearing
Diabetes
Disfigurement, for example, disfigurement caused by burns, wounds, accidents, or congenital disorders
Epilepsy or other seizure disorder
Gastrointestinal disorders, for example, Crohn's Disease, irritable bowel syndrome
Intellectual or developmental disability
Mental health conditions, for example, depression, bipolar disorder, anxiety disorder, schizophrenia, PTSD
Missing limbs or partially missing limbs
Mobility impairment, benefiting from the use of a wheelchair, scooter, walker, leg brace(s) and/or other supports
Nervous system condition, for example, migraine headaches, Parkinson’s disease, multiple sclerosis (MS)
Neurodivergence, for example, attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), autism spectrum disorder, dyslexia, dyspraxia, other learning disabilities
Partial or complete paralysis (any cause)
Pulmonary or respiratory conditions, for example, tuberculosis, asthma, emphysema
Short stature (dwarfism)
Traumatic brain injury

coverLetter:
Hery Bodiwala and the GHD Hiring Team 

 Dallas, TX 

 Subject: Application for Technical Director - Water 

 Dear Hery Bodiwala and the GHD Hiring Team, 

 It is with genuine enthusiasm that I submit my application for the Technical Director - Water position at GHD. Having spent over two decades navigating complex engineering challenges and leading high-performing teams, I am drawn to GHD's profound commitment to addressing critical global challenges in water, energy, and communities. As a former colleague from 7-Eleven, I am particularly motivated by the opportunity to contribute my leadership journey to a company that values expertise and collaboration, and I am excited to explore how my extensive engineering and leadership background can support your mission. 

 My career journey has been centered on transforming ambitious visions into robust, scalable engineering realities, from leading technology initiatives at 7-Eleven to driving critical infrastructure projects in highly regulated environments. My recent immersion in advanced technological innovation, including programs like MIT's, has further sharpened my capabilities in complex systems thinking and leveraging data for enhanced efficiency and resilience. These experiences, though diverse, underscore the core principles of my executive leadership — strategic planning, multi-disciplinary team management, end-to-end project delivery, fostering innovation, and driving operational excellence — which are universally applicable. I am eager to apply this proven leadership acumen to the vital water sector, leveraging my experience in complex engineering solutions design and data-driven decision-making to enhance GHD's impactful projects. 

 The opportunity to lead the design of innovative engineering solutions, manage significant project portfolios, and provide senior strategic leadership in growing the water business within the US Central Region deeply resonates with my professional aspirations. My dedication to mentoring junior engineers, establishing robust engineering standards, and cultivating a culture of innovation aligns perfectly with the values you seek in a Technical Director. I am particularly impressed by GHD's 'One GHD' approach to collaboration and believe my leadership style, which emphasizes inclusion and knowledge sharing, will thrive within your dynamic global network. 

 I am confident that my executive leadership experience, coupled with my passion for contributing to critical global infrastructure, makes me a compelling candidate for this role. I am excited by the prospect of collaborating with your distinguished team to create positive water outcomes that respect nature and nurture communities. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

Job poster

Ask about job
About the job

Job Description

The importance of water to the health of our world can’t be overstated. 

Water is essential for all living organisms to survive and thrive. The water industry must continue to build resiliency and lead from the front to preserve this most essential resource. Together with our clients, we are thinking and doing things differently to help pre-empt and prepare for what's next in the water industry. Be a part of this dynamic and committed team of diverse water specialists and drive change for your community.

You’ll be able to apply our powerful global network of skills and decades of experience to create positive water outcomes that respect nature and nurture communities.

Who are we looking for? 

Technical Director - Water for our US Central operations.

GHD is looking for an energetic, creative, motivated senior engineer to work on existing challenging projects and to join our dynamic and growing water group. Our diversified projects vary from upgrades of existing water and wastewater treatment plants, design of new pump stations, master municipal planning, asset management, linear infrastructure, and water resources. Our growing client list includes some of the largest utilities in our region, as well as many smaller agencies and private sector clients. Utilizing a “One GHD” approach to collaboration, we leverage industry-leading talent from across the globe to provide our clients with unmatched expertise and service.

The ideal candidate should have a proven track record of leading water/wastewater treatment projects, backed by technical experience in design, evaluation and upgrading of medium to large municipal and industrial water and wastewater treatment plants. A demonstrated ability to manage multi-disciplinary teams from concept through detailed design and construction is required, as well as business development skills and the ability to interact professionally with public and private sector clients as a technical leader and subject matter expert.

Working with an energetic and high performing team, this position offers a variety of work and will see you involved in: 


 Needs Assessment: Engage with senior business leaders to understand long-term strategic challenges. 
 Improvement/Innovation: Initiate, formulate, and implement new business practices within a specific discipline while managing the development and/or delivery of a significant element of the organization's change management program. 
 Culture of Innovation: Define the execution plan to disseminate innovative capabilities across the businesses, helping people develop these competencies and enabling them to innovate through solutions such as idea generation platforms, jam sessions, and hackathons. 
 Engineering Solutions Design: Lead the design of complex and/or innovative engineering solutions and the associated validation processes for a business-critical area of engineering to enable the realization of engineering design briefs that conform to organizational and/or regulatory standards. 
 Engineering Standards Specification: Provide leadership on the design and development of engineering standards and specifications for a business-critical area that must be adhered to by the organization and/or its suppliers, contractors, and consultants to ensure engineering work of the required quality is delivered and to manage the risks associated with engineering programs. 
 Knowledge Management: Manage the development of policies and processes for knowledge management to ensure the creation of best practices and the setup of case studies and internal knowledge-sharing sessions; manage, capture, and share knowledge among colleagues on project/program outcomes and innovative practices across a large area to support the practice. 
 Technical Developments Recommendation: Lead the discussion and recommendation of more complex or innovative technical developments to improve the quality of the website/portal/application software and supporting infrastructure to better meet users’ needs. 
 Professional Supervision: Lead and oversee the provision of professional supervision to ensure that there is effective professional supervision that supports practitioners' professional development and embeds reflective practice. 
 Feasibility Studies: Conduct the most-complex feasibility studies from a technological and organizational perspective and document findings to complete cost-benefit analysis on implementing changes to business processes, products, or business unit structure. 
 Product and Solution Development: Take responsibility for managing the definition, development, and delivery of a significant product or service within the product development and engineering program, ensuring alignment with customer requirements. 
 Project Management: Manage a portfolio of major projects in line with overall project management strategy. 
 Client & Customer Management (External): Manage key client and customer relationships. Likely to involve using account teams to maintain customer satisfaction and loyalty.

coverLetter:
Tim Jonas and the Harnham Hiring Team 

 Dallas, TX 

 Subject: Application for Software Engineering Manager - Full Stack 

 Dear Tim Jonas and the Harnham Hiring Team, 

 My journey as an Engineering Leader, spanning over two decades, has been defined by a passion for building robust, scalable software systems and leading high-performing teams, making me particularly enthusiastic about the Software Engineering Manager – Full Stack opportunity. The emphasis on delivering scalable, cross-functional technology solutions for financial workflows, alongside driving modernization and data management improvements, deeply resonates with my professional journey in complex, mission-critical environments. I am confident my comprehensive full-stack expertise and leadership experience align perfectly with Harnham's strategic engineering initiatives for your financial services partner. 

 Throughout my career, particularly during my time at 7-Eleven and CVS Health, I've consistently championed the development and deployment of enterprise-grade software that enhances operational efficiency and strengthens governance. At 7-Eleven, I led teams in building high-scale customer-facing systems and modernizing cloud architectures, directly mirroring your focus on process automation and technology modernization. Similarly, my experience at CVS Health involved managing highly regulated, data-intensive systems, which cultivated a keen understanding of data quality, lineage, and governance—all critical for supporting financial and regulatory objectives within a leading financial services organization. My recent engagement with the MIT Agentic AI program further underscores my commitment to leveraging emerging technologies for impactful solutions. 

 I possess extensive hands-on experience across the full software development lifecycle, including robust CI/CD and deployment practices. My proficiency extends to modern full-stack frameworks like React and Spring Boot, strong compiled language programming in Java and C++, and deep expertise in SQL databases, data modeling, and developing data pipelines, leveraging platforms like Databricks and Snowflake. I thrive in collaborative settings, adept at partnering with diverse stakeholders, from finance and risk to compliance, to translate intricate business requirements into precise technical solutions and establish best practices across engineering. I am eager to apply this expertise to drive your shared services and contribute to the team's success in Dallas. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

About the job

Software Engineering Manager – Full Stack

Dallas, Texas - 5 days/week in-office

$180,000-200,000 base + bonus + LTI




The Company

Harnham is partnering with a leading financial services organization, seeking a Software Engineering Manager to join its Shared Services Engineering team. This centralized engineering group supports several business functions by delivering scalable, cross-functional technology solutions. The team focuses on improving operational efficiency, strengthening governance, and advancing strategic engineering initiatives. Key priorities include process automation, technology modernization, data management improvements, and the development of shared platforms that support business and regulatory objectives.




Responsibilities

Work closely with finance, risk, and compliance stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into technical solutions.
Design, develop, and deploy scalable software systems supporting financial workflows, analytics, and reporting.
Own the full software development lifecycle, including requirements gathering, architecture design, coding, testing, UAT, and maintenance.
Drive modernization and automation initiatives across legacy systems to improve efficiency and scalability.
Develop data models, data pipelines, and analytics solutions ensuring strong data quality, lineage, and governance.
Collaborate across teams to build shared services and establish best practices across engineering.
Leverage emerging technologies and cloud platforms to enhance system capabilities and performance.




Skills And Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a related technical field.
8+ years of experience in software development with strong understanding of data structures, algorithms, and system design.
Hands-on experience across the full development lifecycle, including CI/CD and deployment practices.
Strong programming experience in at least one compiled language such as Java or C++.
Full-stack development experience with modern frameworks and tools (e.g., React, Spring Boot, DropWizard, RESTful APIs, Maven).
Strong experience working with SQL databases, including relational and columnar systems and database design.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to thrive in fast-paced, global environments.
Experience leading or managing small to mid-sized engineering teams preferred.
Experience in data modeling, data warehousing, and analytics solutions is highly desirable.




Preferred Experience

Experience within financial services, particularly supporting front-office, finance, or accounting teams.
Experience building and deploying solutions on cloud-based analytics platforms such as Databricks or Snowflake.
Familiarity with data catalog tools such as Unity Catalog, Open Catalog, or Apache Polaris.




Benefits

The compensation package includes a competitive base salary, performance-based bonus, long-term incentives, and a comprehensive benefits package.




How To Apply

Please submit your CV via the Apply link on this page to register your interest.




Key Terms

Software Engineering | Full Stack | Data Engineering | Financial Services | Risk | Compliance | Controllers | Treasury | Cloud | Databricks | Snowflake | Data Modeling | Data Quality | Automation | Java | React | REST APIs | SQL | Enterprise Systems | C++

Medical insurance, Vision insurance, Dental insurance, 401(k)

competencies: \item \textbf{Engineering Leadership and Team Growth:} Lead, mentor, and grow globally distributed engineering teams, fostering a culture of technical excellence, accountability, and continuous improvement for high-performing outcomes.\item \textbf{Scalable Distributed Systems Architecture:} Own the architecture and technical direction for core profile and provider systems, designing and evolving scalable microservices and high-availability applications.\item \textbf{Platform Modernization and Evolution:} Drive modernization efforts and strategically support critical legacy systems, ensuring continuous innovation, system reliability, and robust operational excellence.\item \textbf{Microservices and API Development:} Expert in building and operating Golang microservices on Kubernetes (Istio), leveraging gRPC and GraphQL APIs for secure, performant, and distributed system design.\item \textbf{Cloud Infrastructure (AWS):} Deep knowledge of AWS cloud infrastructure, including Lambda, EventBridge, and CDK, enabling scalable, resilient, and cost-effective cloud-native application deployment.\item \textbf{Full-Stack Product Delivery:} Lead teams in delivering high-impact, user-facing features across diverse tech stacks, including Java, React, and Node.js for comprehensive product development.\item \textbf{Data Management and Streaming Platforms:} Proficient with data platforms like Kafka, MySQL, and Elasticsearch, crucial for processing, storing, and analyzing high-volume user and provider data.\item \textbf{Agile Delivery and Cross-functional Collaboration:} Champion fast-paced, iterative agile environments, partnering closely with Product and Design to deliver impactful solutions with evolving requirements.
coverLetter:
Hiring Team Care.com 

 Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex, TX 

 Subject: Application for Sr. Engineering Manager 

 Dear Hiring Team Care.com, 

 I am writing to express my enthusiastic interest in the Sr. Engineering Manager position at Care.com. With over two decades of software engineering experience, including significant leadership roles in building and scaling high-performing teams and critical distributed systems, I am deeply aligned with your mission to connect families with great care. My career journey has been dedicated to owning technical direction, driving modernization, and fostering environments that deliver reliable, high-impact user experiences, all of which resonate strongly with the requirements of this role. 

 My experience leading engineering at 7-Eleven, where I spearheaded the creation of scalable consumer-facing platforms, and at CVS Health, managing critical health information systems, directly parallels the challenges and opportunities at Care.com. These roles have equipped me with a robust understanding of how to establish identity, build trust through technology, and ensure seamless engagement across complex platforms. I excel at balancing the evolution of legacy systems with strategic modernization efforts, consistently upholding high standards for code quality and operational excellence to support millions of users. 

 I am passionate about mentoring engineers and raising the technical bar, fostering collaborative environments where teams can thrive and innovate. My expertise spans cloud-native architectures (AWS), microservices, and leading full-stack development using technologies like Java, React, and Node.js. Furthermore, my commitment to continuous learning, exemplified by programs like the MIT Agentic AI curriculum, reinforces my dedication to tackling complex engineering challenges with forward-thinking solutions that drive product impact. I am eager to contribute my leadership and technical acumen to Care.com's vital mission. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi
jobDescription:
Sr. Engineering Manager
Full-time
Compensation: USD 175,000 - USD 200,000 - yearly
Company Description

Care.com is a consumer tech company with heart. We’re on a mission to solve a human challenge we all face: finding great care for the ones we love. We’re moms and dads and pet parents. We have parents and grandparents, so we understand that everyone, at some point in their lives, could use a helping hand. Our culture and our products reflect that.

Job Description

We are a seeking a Sr. Engineering Manager to lead our Provider Experience and User Profile teams,owning the core systems that power how caregivers and families establish identity, build trust, and engage across our platform.

You’ll lead a high-performing, agile team responsible for designing and evolving scalable, distributed systems at the heart of our marketplace. Partnering closely with Product and Design, you’ll deliver reliable, high-impact user experiences while driving modernization and maintaining critical legacy systems. This role is ideal for a leader who values both technical excellence and product impact and understands that the why is just as important as the how.

What You'll Do:

Lead, mentor, and grow a distributed team of engineers
Own the architecture and technical direction of core profile and provider systems
Drive system design, scalability, reliability, and performance improvements
Partner cross-functionally to deliver impactful, user-facing features
Balance modernization efforts with ongoing support and evolution of legacy systems
Establish and uphold high standards for code quality, testing, and operational excellence
Tech Stack: Golang microservices on Kubernetes (Istio), gRPC & GraphQL APIs, AWS (Lambda, EventBridge, CDK), Kafka, MySQL, Elasticsearch, Java, React, and Node.js.
Qualifications
8+ years of software engineering experience, including 2+ years in engineering management
Strong experience building and operating microservices and distributed systems at scale
Deep knowledge of cloud infrastructure (AWS) and modern system architecture patterns
Experience leading the design and delivery of scalable, high-availability applications
Ability to lead and grow high-performing, globally distributed teams
Strong collaboration skills across product, design, and engineering
Experience working in fast-paced, iterative environments with evolving requirements
Passion for mentoring engineers and raising the technical bar
Strong familiarity with our tech stack: Golang microservices on Kubernetes (Istio), gRPC & GraphQL APIs, AWS (Lambda, EventBridge, CDK), Kafka, MySQL, Elasticsearch, Java, React, and Node.js.
Additional Information

Available in more than 20 countries, Care.com is the world's leading platform for finding and managing high-quality family care. Care.com is designed to meet the evolving needs of today's families and caregivers, offering everything from household tax and payroll services and customized corporate benefits packages covering the care needs of working families to innovating new ways for caregivers to be paid and obtain professional benefits. Since 2007, families have relied on Care.com's industry-leading products—from child and elder care to pet care and home care.

Salary Range: $175,000 - $200,000 The base salary range above represents the anticipated low and high end of the national salary range for this position. Actual salaries may vary and may be above or below the range based on various factors including but not limited to work location, experience, and performance. The range listed is just one component of Care.com’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include annual bonuses and short- and long-term incentives. In addition, Care.com provides a variety of benefits to employees, including health insurance coverage, life, and disability insurance, a generous 401K employer matching program, paid holidays, and paid time off (PTO).

coverLetter:
Hiring Team NMI 

 Remote, US 

 Subject: Application for Engineering Manager 

 Dear Hiring Team NMI, 

 My career journey spanning over two decades in software engineering leadership has consistently revolved around building and nurturing high-performing teams, delivering strategic technical roadmaps, and steering organizations through pivotal technological shifts. The Engineering Manager role at NMI, with its emphasis on leading diverse software teams in a security-conscious payments environment and driving AI tool adoption, resonates deeply with my core strengths and aspirations. I am particularly drawn to NMI's mission of democratizing payments technology and empowering innovation for fintech startups. 

 Throughout my tenure leading engineering teams at companies like 7-Eleven, I've gained extensive experience managing complex blended teams of contractors and full-time staff, much like the structure described at NMI. My focus has always been on developing engineers, fostering team health, and making critical decisions regarding technical debt and platform investments to ensure long-term stability while meeting aggressive delivery goals. I thrive as a sounding board for tech leads, credibly challenging architectural decisions and ensuring sound engineering judgment, which is paramount in a highly regulated payments business. 

 Furthermore, my recent advanced studies and experience in Agentic AI and LLM application deployment directly align with NMI's initiative to drive AI tool adoption and challenge engineers to innovate. I am passionate about leading teams through such transformative change, empowering them to work in new, more effective ways. My leadership style, centered on influence rather than direct authorship, is well-suited to guide and empower your tech leads and Product Owners. 

 I am confident that my blend of strategic leadership, technical depth, and experience in cultivating exceptional engineering talent will be a significant asset to NMI. I am eager to contribute to your success in enabling more payments in more ways and places. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi
jobDescription:
Back to jobs
New
Engineering Manager
Remote, US
Apply

We are seeking an Engineering Manager to lead 2–3 software teams — roughly 12–20 engineers in total — each made up of a mix of contractors and full-time staff, including Product Owners and tech leads. This role isn't a hands-on coding position. It's about the people and the direction: growing engineers, keeping the teams healthy and focused, and steering decisions about technical debt and platform investment.

You'll be the person tech leads bring their thinking to — a sounding board who can pressure-test an approach, and just as importantly, push back when something is being over-engineered or over-thought. You'll own the people side of your teams (development, performance, retention, hiring) and represent their work and capacity to stakeholders across the organization. You'll also lead the teams through change — including driving the adoption of AI tools and challenging engineers to work in new, more effective ways.

As a payments business, we operate to rigorous security and regulatory standards, so sound engineering judgment carries real weight here. The best fit is someone who has managed engineers before, has enough technical depth to challenge engineering decisions credibly, and is comfortable leading through influence rather than authorship.


Key Responsibilities:

Manage and develop multiple teams of engineers across a wide seniority range, supporting their growth, performance, and career direction.
Lead blended teams of contractors and full-time staff, keeping each group focused, healthy, and productive.
Lead the teams through change — including driving AI tool adoption and challenging engineers to adapt how they work.
Set and defend direction on technical debt and platform investment, balancing long-term health against delivery pressure.
Act as a sounding board for tech leads — pressure-test their thinking and challenge over-engineering or over-thinking before it costs the teams time.
Partner with Product Owners and tech leads to keep delivery aligned, realistic, and well-prioritized.
Manage stakeholder relationships, communicating team capacity, progress, and trade-offs clearly.
Recruit and retain strong engineers, and build teams people want to work on.

Skills & Experience:
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field — or equivalent industry experience.
Proven experience managing software engineers, including senior engineers and a mix of contractors and full-time staff.
Experience delivering software in a regulated, security-conscious environment such as payments or financial services (familiarity with standards like PCI DSS, HIPAA, SOX, GDPR, or CCPA is an advantage).
A track record of leading teams through change, including adopting new tools and ways of working.
Enough technical depth to credibly challenge architecture and engineering decisions — though this is not a coding or code-review role.
A track record of leading through influence: guiding tech leads and POs rather than directing the work yourself.
Experience working with remote and globally distributed teams.
Strong communication and stakeholder-management skills.

As well as being a part of something exciting everyday, you will also receive the following benefits:
Competitive salary + bonus 
A remote first culture!
Flex PTO 
Health, Dental and Vision Insurance
13 Paid Holidays
Company volunteer days

What we do!

NMI enables our partners with choice, and challenges the one-size-fits-all approach to payments. You've probably used NMI in the last 24 hours without even realizing it. We’re the platform that powers success for innovative tech created by SMBs, entrepreneurs and fintech startups. We’re creative problem solvers who help visionaries smash through boundaries and think beyond what’s possible so they can think about what’s next. But we’re not just built for the tech savvy. We democratize the latest payments technology so that everyone can realize the benefits of easy payments across the full spectrum of commerce. We’re all about enabling more payments in more ways and more places.

We believe that having a diverse group of employees strengthens both our work and our workplace. We’re focused on making NMI more diverse and welcoming with initiatives like having a dedicated Diversity, Equity & Inclusion action group, diversity goals for hiring, anonymized resume screening, affinity groups such as our Women's network and LGBTQ+ Network, open forums for discussions on diversity and social justice, and measuring inclusion and belonging as part of our regular employee engagement surveys.

Equal Opportunity

NMI is committed to providing equal employment opportunity for all persons regardless of race, color, religion, sex, age, marital status, national origin, sexual orientation or sexual identity, genetic information, citizen status (except those that do not have the legal right to be employed in the United States), disability, military service, service member, veteran status, or any other basis protected by applicable law.

Please be aware that all offers of employment are made subject to receipt of satisfactory background and financial checks.

Attention job applicants: Please note that in compliance with the data protection regulations within your jurisdiction, any personal information submitted with your job application may be collected and used by NMI for the purpose of recruitment and employment-related activities. By submitting your application, you acknowledge and provide explicit consent to the processing of your personal information as described in our privacy policy found on our website. For more information on how we process your information, please read our privacy policy here: https://www.nmi.com/legal/privacy-policy/

Why are you being asked to complete this form?

We are a federal contractor or subcontractor. The law requires us to provide equal employment opportunity to qualified people with disabilities. We have a goal of having at least 7% of our workers as people with disabilities. The law says we must measure our progress towards this goal. To do this, we must ask applicants and employees if they have a disability or have ever had one. People can become disabled, so we need to ask this question at least every five years.

Completing this form is voluntary, and we hope that you will choose to do so. Your answer is confidential. No one who makes hiring decisions will see it. Your decision to complete the form and your answer will not harm you in any way. coverLetter:
Hiring Team Fuze Health 

 Remote, United States 

 Subject: Application for Sr. Engineering Manager 

 Dear Hiring Team Fuze Health, 

 My career journey spanning over two decades in engineering leadership has consistently focused on building high-performing teams and delivering secure, scalable software solutions that make a tangible impact. I am deeply impressed by Fuze Health's mission to simplify access to medications, improve patient adherence, and streamline the digital care journey, and I see a compelling alignment with my expertise in driving innovation and operational excellence in complex, regulated environments. 

 My experience at CVS Health provided invaluable insights into the intricacies of digital health and navigating highly regulated healthcare environments, ensuring robust patient data security, privacy, and compliance with standards such as HIPAA and SOC2. Concurrently, my leadership roles, including at 7-Eleven, honed my ability to architect and scale B2B SaaS and consumer-facing web applications, particularly complex logistical and transactional systems that are directly analogous to FuzeRx's API-driven fulfillment platforms. I thrive on leading multiple engineering squads, guiding technical vision, and translating strategic objectives into precise, executable roadmaps. 

 Technically, I bring a deep architectural understanding and hands-on experience in building and deploying systems leveraging AWS, PostgreSQL, Python, alongside modern web frameworks like Ruby on Rails, TypeScript, and JavaScript—all central to your technology stack. I am adept at owning the end-to-end SDLC, implementing CI/CD pipeline optimization, and establishing key engineering metrics to foster a culture of continuous improvement and system reliability, ensuring that our solutions are not just functional but truly exemplary in performance and security. 

 I am eager to contribute my leadership, technical acumen, and passion for improving patient outcomes to Fuze Health. I am confident that my background uniquely positions me to drive the architectural and delivery excellence required for this pivotal role. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi
jobDescription:
Back to jobs
Sr. Engineering Manager
Remote, United States
Apply

At Fuze Health, we put patients first and tirelessly address the most pressing needs in healthcare. We empower millions to digitally connect with care providers, essential health resources and needed treatments – and enable care providers, employers, health plans and life sciences companies to meaningfully enhance quality, outcomes and value. We are dedicated to helping our partners evolve and modernize to meet emerging patient and marketplace needs.

Fuze Health’s foundation is built upon the strategic combination of several proven, technology-powered innovators in the digital health, diagnostics, and pharmacy sectors. Our growing portfolio brings together the capabilities of industry leaders including LetsGetChecked, Truepill, and Alto Pharmacy, to create a distinctive, unified force in healthcare. Together, we have the shared vision, advanced capabilities and talented teams to deliver next-generation solutions that patients and healthcare partners need today and into the future. 

Job Description:

As the Senior Manager of Engineering at Fuze Health/FuzeRx, you are a pivotal leader within our technology organization, bridging the gap between high-level strategic healthcare objectives and ground-level technical execution. This role sits within our core product engineering hierarchy, reporting directly to the VP of Engineering. You will lead multiple engineering squads, driving the architecture, development, and delivery of secure, scalable software solutions that power our digital pharmacy and API-driven fulfillment platforms. By fostering a culture of operational excellence and engineering rigor leveraging AWS, PostgreSQL, Rails, and Python, this role directly contributes to our mission of simplifying access to medications, improving patient adherence, and streamlining the digital care journey.

Key Responsibilities:
Team Leadership & Development: Recruit, mentor, and manage a team of 10+ software engineers and engineering managers across multiple agile squads, fostering a culture of continuous learning, inclusivity, and high performance.
Technical Strategy & Architecture: Guide the technical vision for FuzeRx’s core B2B and consumer-facing platforms, ensuring highly available and secure solutions leveraging our primary tech stack (Ruby on Rails, Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, PostgreSQL, AWS).
Delivery & Execution: Own the end-to-end software development lifecycle (SDLC) for your domain, ensuring predictable delivery of features that drive API-powered prescription fulfillment and pharmacy logistics.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner closely with Product Management, Pharmacy Operations, Design, and QA to translate complex healthcare compliance and patient-care requirements into technical roadmaps and actionable sprints.
Regulatory & Security Compliance: Champion engineering best practices that adhere to strict industry regulations (e.g., HIPAA, SOC2), ensuring patient data security, secure messaging, and privacy are woven into the fabric of our codebase.
Operational Excellence: Establish and track key engineering metrics (e.g., DORA metrics, system uptime, bug resolution rates) to drive continuous improvement in engineering efficiency and pharmacy system reliability.
Incident Management: Serve as a senior escalation point for critical system outages or security incidents, leading root-cause analyses and implementing preventive measures.
Resource Allocation: Manage departmental budgets, vendor relationships, and cloud infrastructure costs effectively to support sustainable scale.
Required Experience & Qualifications:
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field
Technical Experience: 8+ years of progressive experience in software engineering, architecture, and distributed systems design
Management Experience: 3+ years of direct people management experience, including managing managers or leading multiple concurrent engineering teams
Industry Experience: Previous experience building scalable B2B SaaS or consumer-facing web applications, with a strong understanding of complex logistical or transactional systems
Shift / Site Specific Requirements: Standard business hours; available for on-call escalations as needed
Tools & Tech Stack: Deep architectural understanding and proven experience deploying systems using AWS, PostgreSQL, Ruby on Rails, Python, TypeScript, and JavaScript. Familiarity with collaboration and procurement tools (e.g., Slack, GSuite, Coupa, Jira)
Leadership Skills: Proven ability to build high-trust relationships, manage conflict, and communicate complex technical concepts clearly to non-technical stakeholders (e.g., clinical and operational leaders)
Objective Expertise: Demonstrable expertise in Agile/Scrum methodologies, CI/CD pipeline optimization, and relational database architecture
Preferred Qualifications:
Education: Master’s degree in Computer Science or an MBA with a technology focus
Industry Experience: Prior engineering leadership experience within Pharmacy Tech, Digital Health, or highly regulated environments
Domain Expertise: Deep understanding of healthcare data interoperability, pharmacy management systems, API-led healthcare integrations, or electronic health record (EHR) integrations
Measurable Expertise: Proven track record of successfully scaling engineering teams by 50%+ during high-growth phases, or leading major architecture modernization projects within an AWS ecosystem

Physical requirements for this role include the ability to work at a computer terminal with monitor, keyboard and mouse for extended periods of time, stoop, bend, and reach for equipment and supplies, make frequent repetitive motions required to operate a computer that include the wrists, hands and fingers, and lift, carry, push, pull, and move light objects up to 20 pounds. The role also requires the ability to effectively communicate through verbal interactions, discern auditory information, and visually perceive details to perform essential job functions.

Consistent with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and similar applicable state laws, it is Fuze Health’s policy to provide reasonable accommodation to enable qualified individuals with disabilities to perform essential job functions, unless such accommodation would cause an undue hardship.

Salary Range: $182,400 - $228,000

Location Requirement: 

FuzeRx (Truepill / Fuze Care Services) is limited to individuals residing in the following states: Alabama, Arizona, Arkansas, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Florida, Georgia, Hawaii, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Maryland, Massachusetts, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Nevada, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, North Carolina, Ohio, Oklahoma, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Vermont, Virginia, Washington (WA), Wisconsin.

Employment Authorization Requirement: Applicants must be authorized to work for any employer in the U.S. Alto Pharmacy, Fuze Health, LetsGetChecked, and Fuze Care Services can not take over, transfer, or sponsor any visa type at this time.

Full-time employee benefits include: dental, vision, and multiple group medical plans to choose from, a 401(k) retirement savings plan, group life insurance, accidental death and dismemberment (AD&D) insurance, flexible spending account (FSA) and health savings account (HSA), commuter benefits, employer-paid short-term (STD) and long-term disability (LTD) insurance, and additional supplemental insurance plans (spouse life insurance, legal insurance, an employee assistance program, home health testing kits, and a fertility medication discount program). Employees are also provided flexible vacation time, accrued paid sick time, 10 paid holidays, (2 floating holidays for full time non-exempt employees) , and eight weeks of paid parental leave for eligible employees, additional paid weeks for the birthing parent, 4 weeks paid caregiver leave, and a Lifestyle Spending Account allowance each month.

Why are you being asked to complete this form?

We are a federal contractor or subcontractor. The law requires us to provide equal employment opportunity to qualified people with disabilities. We have a goal of having at least 7% of our workers as people with disabilities. The law says we must measure our progress towards this goal. To do this, we must ask applicants and employees if they have a disability or have ever had one. People can become disabled, so we need to ask this question at least every five years.

Completing this form is voluntary, and we hope that you will choose to do so. Your answer is confidential. No one who makes hiring decisions will see it. Your decision to complete the form and your answer will not harm you in any way. If you want to learn more about the law or this form, visit the U.S. Department of Labor’s Office of Federal Contract Compliance Programs (OFCCP) website at www.dol.gov/ofccp.

How do you know if you have a disability?

A disability is a condition that substantially limits one or more of your “major life activities.” If you have or have ever had such a condition, you are a person with a disability. Disabilities include, but are not limited to:

Alcohol or other substance use disorder (not currently using drugs illegally)
Autoimmune disorder, for example, lupus, fibromyalgia, rheumatoid arthritis, HIV/AIDS
Blind or low vision
Cancer (past or present)
Cardiovascular or heart disease
Celiac disease
Cerebral palsy
Deaf or serious difficulty hearing
Diabetes
Disfigurement, for example, disfigurement caused by burns, wounds, accidents, or congenital disorders
Epilepsy or other seizure disorder
Gastrointestinal disorders, for example, Crohn's Disease, irritable bowel syndrome
Intellectual or developmental disability
Mental health conditions, for example, depression, bipolar disorder, anxiety disorder, schizophrenia, PTSD
Missing limbs or partially missing limbs
Mobility impairment, benefiting from the use of a wheelchair, scooter, walker, leg brace(s) and/or other supports
Nervous system condition, for example, migraine headaches, Parkinson’s disease, multiple sclerosis (MS)
Neurodivergence, for example, attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), autism spectrum disorder, dyslexia, dyspraxia, other learning disabilities
Partial or complete paralysis (any cause)
Pulmonary or respiratory conditions, for example, tuberculosis, asthma, emphysema
Short stature (dwarfism)
Traumatic brain injury

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Providence 

 Plainview, TX 

 Subject: Application for Senior Manager - End Point Digital Experience Team - Remote 

 Dear Hiring Team Providence, 

 I am writing to express my profound interest in the Senior Manager - End Point Digital Experience Team position. With over two decades of engineering leadership experience, I have consistently focused on building and scaling high-performing teams to deliver exceptional digital experiences. My journey, including leadership roles at organizations like 7-Eleven and CVS Health, has deeply ingrained a customer-centric and operational excellence mindset, which I believe translates directly to enhancing the digital experience for Providence's invaluable caregivers. This hybrid role, at the intersection of endpoint engineering, analytics, and service experience, perfectly aligns with my expertise in transforming technical challenges into seamless, intuitive solutions. 

 My background is rich with instances of translating complex data into actionable strategies, a core requirement for this role. At CVS Health, I led initiatives that leveraged advanced analytics to drive significant operational improvements, mirroring the need to design and operationalize endpoint telemetry and insights at Providence. I am adept at building executive-ready dashboards and using data proactively to identify degradation and optimization opportunities, ensuring that technology's performance and stability directly support critical clinical workflows and business outcomes within highly regulated environments. 

 Furthermore, my leadership approach emphasizes cultivating engineering rigor, fostering cross-functional alignment, and championing automation and modern service engineering principles. I am passionate about improving how technology actually feels and performs for users, driving standardization while strategically allowing for necessary exceptions, and implementing robust incident response and problem management. My continuous learning journey underscores my commitment to innovation and leadership in complex, matrixed organizations like Providence, where empowering caregivers through superior digital tools is paramount. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

About the job

Description

Senior Manager- End Point Digital Experience - Remote

This role is a senior, hybrid role at the intersection of endpoint engineering, analytics, and service experience. This role is responsible for designing, operating, and continuously improving the digital experience of caregiver endpoints—from clinical thin clients and thick clients to virtual desktops, mobile devices, printers and enterprise workstations.

Working in close partnership with developers, service engineers, clinicians, operations teams, and program managers, this role introduces and scales a modern service‑engineering and experience‑driven mindset across Endpoint Services. The focus is not just system uptime—but how technology actually feels and performs for caregivers delivering care.

This role requires a leader who can thrive in ambiguity, translate data into action, coach others, and drive clear strategies that balance technical excellence with clinical and business outcomes.

Digital Experience & Satisfaction


Own and continuously improve caregiver digital experience across endpoint platforms (thin clients, thick clients, print, mobile, peripherals).
Define and measure experience KPIs (performance, reliability, responsiveness, satisfaction).
Partner with clinical and operational leaders to ensure endpoint services support real clinical workflows.
Leverage sentiment, satisfaction data, and experience analytics to prioritize engineering improvements.


Analytics, Telemetry & Insights


Design and operationalize endpoint telemetry and analytics to provide real‑time and trend‑based insights into performance, stability, and usage.
Correlate telemetry signals (CPU, memory, latency, login times, app health) with caregiver experience and business impact.
Build dashboards and executive‑ready insights that translate technical data into clear outcomes and decisions.
Use data to proactively identify degradation, experience risks, and optimization opportunities before they impact care.


Endpoint & Platform Engineering


Engineer and support modern endpoint platforms including thin clients, thick clients, virtual desktops, and hybrid environments.
Partner with software engineering teams to improve endpoint performance, reliability, and lifecycle automation.
Drive standardization while allowing for clinically justified exceptions.
Support zero‑touch provisioning, self‑service capabilities, and automation across endpoint services.


Service Engineering & Operations


Apply service engineering principles to design, scale, and run enterprise endpoint services.
Partner early with development and infrastructure teams to ensure services are built with reliability, observability, and experience in mind.
Lead continuous improvement of incident response, root cause analysis, and problem management for endpoint experience issues.
Balance speed, quality, and safety in a highly available clinical environment.


Leadership, Coaching & Strategy


Serve as a technical leader and coach within Endpoint Services, elevating engineering rigor and experience thinking.
Drive cross‑functional alignment between IT, clinical operations, and business stakeholders.
Lead through influence in a complex, matrixed organization.
Help define and execute the long‑term digital experience strategy for enterprise endpoints.


Providence caregivers are not simply valued – they’re invaluable. Join our team at Enterprise Information Services and thrive in our culture of patient-focused, whole-person care built on understanding, commitment, and mutual respect. Your voice matters here, because we know that to inspire and retain the best people, we must empower them.

Required Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in computer engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering.
8 years of related experience; more preferred.
6 years of experience leading technical teams in a complex and fast-moving environment.
Strong experience in leveraging the power of data and metrics to drive behavior, process and priority decisions across the team and IS (Information Services).


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in healthcare, clinical, or highly regulated environments.
Experience with ServiceNow, Snowflake, PowerBI and other analytical tools
Experience with automation, scripting, and software‑based solutions to reduce manual operations.
Demonstrated success improving customer or caregiver satisfaction through technology.
Background in modern service reliability or service engineering practices

Why Join Providence?

Our best-in-class benefits are uniquely designed to support you and your family in staying well, growing professionally, and achieving financial security. We take care of you, so you can focus on delivering our Mission of caring for everyone, especially the most vulnerable in our communities.

Accepting a new position at another facility that is part of the Providence family of organizations may change your current benefits. Changes in benefits, including paid time-off, happen for various reasons. These reasons can include changes of Legal Employer, FTE, Union, location, time-off plan policies, availability of health and welfare benefit plan offerings, and other various reasons.

About Providence

At Providence, our strength lies in Our Promise of “Know me, care for me, ease my way.” Working at our family of organizations means that regardless of your role, we’ll walk alongside you in your career, supporting you so you can support others. We provide best-in-class benefits and we foster an inclusive workplace where diversity is valued, and everyone is essential, heard and respected. Together, our 120,000 caregivers (all employees) serve in over 50 hospitals, over 1,000 clinics and a full range of health and social services across Alaska, California, Montana, New Mexico, Oregon, Texas and Washington. As a comprehensive health care organization, we are serving more people, advancing best practices and continuing our more than 100-year tradition of serving the poor and vulnerable.

coverLetter:
Corey McMahon and the HopSkipDrive Hiring Team 

 United States (Remote) 

 Subject: Application for VP, Engineering 

 Dear Corey McMahon and the HopSkipDrive Hiring Team, 

 I am writing to express my profound interest in the VP, Engineering position at HopSkipDrive. Your mission to create opportunity through safe, reliable student transportation deeply resonates with my journey, and your commitment to being an "AI-forward" company perfectly aligns with my expertise in building and scaling high-performing, AI-accelerated engineering organizations. With over two decades of software leadership experience, I am eager to contribute to a company that embraces innovative technology to solve critical societal challenges. 

 My background includes significant experience leading and scaling engineering teams, much like the 30-60 engineer team described, and fostering a culture of technical excellence and continuous improvement. I have a proven track record of engaging credibly in complex system design and architecture discussions across modern web, mobile, cloud, and marketplace-oriented environments, including those that leverage Ruby on Rails, React, iOS, and Android platforms. Crucially, my recent endeavors and MIT Agentic AI program completion have equipped me with specific expertise in pioneering the integration of AI and agentic capabilities into the software development lifecycle, which I see as a cornerstone for HopSkipDrive's competitive advantage. 

 I am adept at driving operational excellence, ensuring predictable, high-quality execution and world-class reliability, while balancing technical hygiene with critical business needs. My leadership journey has also involved thoughtful change leadership, guiding organizations through strategic shifts with empathy and clarity, and effectively managing fiscal health, including headcount and infrastructure budgets. I am passionate about cultivating product-centric teams that operate with high autonomy, leveraging clear asynchronous communication, which is vital in a remote-first environment. 

 I am confident that my blend of strategic vision, deep technical credibility, and passion for mission-driven work makes me an ideal candidate to lead HopSkipDrive's engineering organization into its next phase of scale and development. I am excited by the prospect of leveraging AI to further enhance your platform and drive meaningful impact for families and schools across the country. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

About the job

At HopSkipDrive, our mission is to create opportunity for all through mobility. We're the leader in safe, fast, and simple supplemental student transportation through our marketplace, we connect kids to highly-vetted caregivers on wheels. Through our software, we solve the biggest transportation challenges facing schools and school districts across the country.

Founded by three moms as a solution to their own transportation challenges, we've now facilitated more than five million rides across over over 20 states. We continue to grow rapidly — earning a spot on the Inc. 5000 list numerous times and the Deloitte 500 Fast-Growing Technology list. HopSkipDrive is a Series D company and has raised $100M to date.

How We Work

We're an AI-forward company, and we expect every person on our team to be too. We use AI tools to do our best work — drafting, analyzing, building, and shipping faster than we could without them — and we invest in training, share what works, and govern AI use thoughtfully. We don't expect you to be an expert when you start. We do expect you to be curious, willing to learn, and ready to use the best tools available to move our mission forward.

We're remote-first, mission-driven, and built for people who want to do work that matters with people who hold a high bar.

Who We Are

HopSkipDrive's Product & Technology organization is the heart of our competitive advantage, building the technologies that have and continue to transform student transportation. Our applications span web and mobile, B2B and B2C, operational and transformational. We embrace the power of AI to allow us to deliver more value to our customers faster, while keeping humans at the center of our work. Our teams operate through shared ownership and close collaboration rather than functional handoffs. We value leaders who can navigate complex tradeoffs across customer experience, operational realities, technical scalability, and long-term platform health.

As the VP of Engineering, you will lead our engineering organization through its next phase of scale and development. Partnering closely with the Chief Product & Technology Officer (CPTO) and the SVP of Product, you will drive execution, foster a high-performance culture, and lead our transition toward an AI-accelerated engineering environment.

Key Responsibilities


Lead and scale a high-performing engineering organization by building deep trust, diagnosing organizational strengths, and establishing the career growth paths and structural changes required to elevate performance.
Engage deeply and credibly in system design and architecture discussions, helping senior engineers navigate complex technical trade-offs across our platform (Ruby on Rails, React, iOS/Android), service boundaries, and modern infrastructure investments.
Pioneer the integration of AI and agentic capabilities into our software development lifecycle, spanning the evaluation of new technical infrastructure, coaching teams through change management, and establishing the workflows, testing methodologies, and QA guardrails required to safely accelerate shipping velocity.
Own the Engineering department's delivery and operational excellence, ensuring predictable, high-quality execution, world-class reliability, and continuous improvement of technical health and process discipline.
Partner closely with the CPTO, SVP of Product, Design, Data, and Operations to align product vision with technical strategy and drive unified customer and business outcomes.
Manage the engineering department's fiscal health, including headcount, contractors, software, and infrastructure budgets.


Who You Are

You are a seasoned engineering leader who has stayed close to the technology and understands how to navigate organizational growth with empathy and clarity. You possess the strategic vision to lead an engineering department into its next phase of maturity while maintaining a deep, credible connection to the architecture and tech stack.

What You Bring


Deep technical credibility rooted in the ability to engage constructively in architecture, system design, and engineering quality discussions as a collaborative peer to senior engineers and technical leaders.
Breadth across modern web, mobile, cloud, and marketplace-oriented environments, with the ability to reason across Ruby on Rails, React, native iOS and Android clients, and modern cloud infrastructure (AWS or GCP).
Experience evolving developer workflows to leverage automation and AI agents, with a fluent understanding of how these tools impact code quality, security, and velocity, and a proven track record of successfully driving developer adoption.
Proven leadership at scale, including experience managing engineering managers, designing organizational structures for teams of 30-60 engineers, and making deliberate talent decisions to optimize team health.
Thoughtful change leadership and the resilience to drive organizational or cultural shifts with empathy, clarity, and a proven ability to bring skeptical teams along.
Business and product centricity, with a history of balancing technical hygiene, reliability, architecture investments, operational realities, and near-term customer and business needs.
Excellent asynchronous communication skills, emphasizing clear written documentation and structured delegation that empowers remote engineering teams to operate with high autonomy.


Our Investment In You

We want you to be an owner in our company and share in executing our vision, so every full-time employee has equity. In addition, we offer flexible vacation, medical, dental, vision and life insurance, 401(k), FSA, and an opportunity to work for a uniquely positioned, VC-backed company in a hugely attractive space with significant upside potential. HopSkipDrive is proud to operate as a drug-free workplace. HopSkipDrive is also committed to fair and equitable compensation practices. The salary range for this role in Los Angeles, CA is $240,000 - $270,000 + bonus. This position is remote and, as such, compensation will ultimately be in line with the location in which the position is filled. Final compensation for this role will be determined by several factors such as a candidate’s relevant work experience, skill set, certifications, and specific work location. The total compensation package for this role also includes equity stock options.

HopSkipDrive is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, veteran status, or any other protected class.


 This role will be fully remote in one of the following states AZ, CA, CO, DC, FL, GA, IL, IN, KS, MA, MD, MI, MN, MO, NC, NJ, NM, NV, NY, OK, OR, SC, TN, TX, UT, VA, WA, WI**

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Vizient, Inc 

 Irving, TX 

 Subject: Application for Director of Engineering 

 Dear Hiring Team Vizient, Inc, 

 My journey in software engineering leadership spans over two decades, marked by a commitment to driving innovation and building exceptional teams, and I am particularly drawn to the Director of Engineering opportunity at Vizient, Inc. Having followed Vizient's impactful work in enhancing healthcare performance, I am excited by the prospect of contributing my leadership experience, including my tenure scaling technology at 7-Eleven and leading strategic initiatives at CVS Health, to your mission. My background aligns strongly with your emphasis on strategic and technical leadership, talent development, and delivering secure, scalable solutions. 

 Throughout my career, I have consistently provided strategic and technical leadership across engineering teams, ensuring delivery excellence and alignment with core enterprise objectives, much like those at Vizient. I have a proven track record of developing comprehensive technical roadmaps, partnering effectively with product and architecture stakeholders, and establishing engineering standards and best practices in matrixed environments. My experience includes designing and deploying large-scale data applications and cultivating high-performing teams, which directly addresses your qualifications for this role. 

 I am particularly adept at fostering inclusive environments where engineers feel empowered to contribute their unique skills and talents, driving organizational effectiveness. My continued pursuit of cutting-edge knowledge, exemplified by my recent completion of the MIT Agentic AI program, underscores my commitment to leveraging technological innovations to solve complex challenges, a capability I believe would greatly benefit Vizient as you continue to expand your market leadership through data-driven insights. I am eager to discuss how my leadership and expertise can contribute to Vizient's continued success and reinforce your position as a leader in healthcare performance improvement. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

coverLetter:
Hiring Team HHAeXchange 

 United States 

 Subject: Application for Director, Software Engineering 

 Dear Hiring Team HHAeXchange, 

 I am writing to express my profound interest in the Director, Software Engineering position at HHAeXchange, a company I admire for its commitment to transforming healthcare through innovative software solutions. With over two decades of experience in software engineering leadership, specializing in scaling high-performing AI/ML teams and delivering complex enterprise SaaS platforms, I am confident my journey aligns perfectly with your mission to enhance patient care and operational efficiency. 

 My background includes leading significant technological advancements at organizations like 7-Eleven, where I oversaw high-scale customer-facing systems, and CVS Health, where I drove digital health initiatives with a strong focus on clinical operational excellence and data governance. This diverse experience has prepared me to tackle the unique challenges of the healthcare IT landscape, ensuring compliance, security, and superior patient outcomes. Furthermore, my recent credentials from the MIT Agentic AI program provide a cutting-edge perspective on integrating advanced AI-enabled capabilities and modern AI-assisted SDLC tools, directly addressing HHAeXchange's push for innovation and engineering effectiveness. 

 I am particularly drawn to your emphasis on cloud modernization, architectural improvements, and driving predictable delivery while fostering a culture of continuous improvement. My expertise spans end-to-end engineering delivery, establishing robust engineering governance, and advancing cloud-native principles using technologies like AWS, Docker, and Kubernetes. I thrive on coaching leaders and individual contributors, fostering a collaborative environment, and translating strategic vision into tangible, high-quality software solutions. I am eager to apply this comprehensive experience to contribute to the success of your Enterprise and Sandata SaaS platforms. 

 Thank you for considering my application. I am enthusiastic about the opportunity to discuss how my leadership, technical acumen, and passion for healthcare innovation can significantly benefit HHAeXchange. I look forward to the possibility of contributing to your remarkable team. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

Message
About the job

HHAeXchange is one of the healthcare industry’s fastest-growing healthcare software solution providers. Founded by professionals with deep experience in software development and healthcare, our platforms support post-acute payers and providers seeking innovative yet cost-effective ways to deliver better service to patients while maintaining compliance with rapidly changing regulations.

We are seeking a Director of Software Engineering to join our R&D organization and provide leadership across teams delivering capabilities for both the HHAeXchange Enterprise platform and Sandata SaaS solutions. This role is responsible for ensuring teams execute consistently across engineering delivery, quality, security, performance, reliability, and production operations. The Director will partner closely with Product, Architecture, QA, and Operations leadership to deliver scalable outcomes while continuously improving platform stability and engineering effectiveness.

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential job duty satisfactorily. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform essential functions.

This is a fully remote opportunity for candidates located in the EST or CST time zones within the US only.

Essential Job Duties


Own engineering delivery, maintenance, and production operations responsibilities for assigned areas across the HHAeXchange SaaS platforms.
Deliver new features and optimize existing components with focus on code quality, scalability, performance, security, stability, and maintainability.
Ensure effective incident response, troubleshooting, and operational execution for production systems.
Establish clear accountability and execution standards across engineering teams.
Provide leadership across multiple engineering teams, including engineering managers, tech leads, and individual contributors.
Drive predictable delivery through strong planning, prioritization, and execution discipline.
Set expectations for performance and accountability across teams.
Coach leaders and engineers through mentorship, feedback, and professional development.
Drive modernization and platform evolution, including cloud and architectural improvements.
Advance cloud-native principles and best practices in new development and enhancements.
Partner with architecture leadership to ensure system designs meet enterprise standards.
Promote continuous improvement through refactoring, technical debt reduction, and platform upgrades.
Lead engineering governance and operational readiness, ensuring consistency and quality across releases.
Participate in or support architecture review and design review processes.
Ensure proper release planning, change management, and production readiness practices.
Establish effective standards for deployment quality, rollback readiness, and operational support coverage.
Drive development and adoption of AI-enabled capabilities across the HHAeXchange SaaS platforms.
Partner with Product, Architecture, and Data/AI stakeholders to identify and deliver high-impact AI use cases that improve operational efficiency, compliance, and customer outcomes.
Ensure AI features are built with strong standards for quality, performance, security, privacy, and reliability.
Lead adoption of AI-assisted SDLC tools and practices to improve engineering velocity and effectiveness (e.g., Cursor and other modern AI coding tools).
Establish best practices, guardrails, and repeatable workflows for AI-supported development, testing, code review, and documentation.
Measure and improve productivity and quality outcomes through responsible use of AI tooling while maintaining engineering standards and maintainability.
Work closely with VP, Software Engineering and CTO to formulate strategies and execute the vision.
Collaborate with Product Managers on future product strategy.
Collaborate and work with the team with a positive and open-minded attitude.
Share domain and technical expertise, providing technical mentorship and cross-training to other peers and team members.
Enable automation of configuration management of development, quality assurance, and production environments as well as automation of continuous integration and continuous delivery.
Supervise training and development of software engineering staff.
Support recruiting engineering personnel, technical staffing, and project teams’ formation.


Other Job Duties


Other duties as assigned by supervisor or HHA exchange leader.



Travel Requirements


Travel up to 10%, including overnight travel



Required Education, Experience, Certifications And Skills


10+ years of experience in developing, deploying, monitoring, and supporting applications.
Minimal 5+ years of experience in a leadership position managing highly skilled software development teams across US and around the globe.
Proven experience leading development of AI-enabled software products, including applying machine learning, automation, or intelligent decisioning to enterprise SaaS platforms.
Experience driving adoption of AI-assisted software development lifecycle (SDLC) tools to improve engineering productivity, quality, and delivery speed (e.g., AI-powered coding, testing, and documentation tools such as Cursor or similar platforms).
Strong understanding of responsible AI practices, including quality, security, privacy, explainability, and maintainability considerations in production systems.
Strong requirements gathering, analysis, and questioning skills.
Strong understanding of software engineering best practices including design, development, testing, release, and operational support.
Experience leading teams that support business-critical enterprise SaaS systems (e.g., healthcare platforms, billing, ERP, workflow systems).
Ability to review and guide engineering execution across designs, code quality, production deployments, performance, and stability improvements.
Familiarity with modern architecture and operational practices including API and service-based architecture, cloud infrastructure, and incident response.
Extensive experience with .NET, Angular, Web Services/APIs, and SQL Server (or comparable enterprise SaaS technology stacks).
Experience with containerization and orchestration technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes (or equivalent platforms).
Experience with AWS cloud services. AWS certification is a plus.
Excellent communication skills and ability to drive outcomes across engineering and cross-functional partners.
Proven ability to lead through ambiguity, manage competing priorities, and deliver predictable results.



The base salary range for this US-based, full-time, and exempt position is $189,000-208,000/yr, not including variable compensation. An employee’s exact starting salary will be based on various factors including but not limited to experience, education, training, merit, location, and the ability to exemplify the HHAeXchange core values.

This is a benefits-eligible position. HHAeXchange offers competitive health plans, paid time-off, company paid holidays, 401K retirement program with a Company elected match, including other company sponsored programs.

HHAeXchange is an equal-opportunity employer. The Company offers employment opportunities to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, marital status, veteran status, citizenship, genetic information, hairstyles, or any other status protected by local or federal law.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Harbor Freight Tools 

 Remote, Calabasas, CA 

 Subject: Application for Senior Manager Engineering Process and Labor Management 

 Dear Hiring Team Harbor Freight Tools, 

 I am writing to express my enthusiastic interest in the Senior Manager Engineering Process and Labor Management position, having followed Harbor Freight Tools' impressive journey of aggressive store expansion and commitment to operational excellence. With over two decades of engineering leadership, particularly in high-volume, dynamic environments like 7-Eleven, I have cultivated a robust expertise in scaling complex systems and driving significant operational efficiencies, which I believe aligns perfectly with the demands of leading your distribution network's engineering and process design initiatives. 

 My career journey has been centered on translating strategic vision into tangible, high-impact solutions, consistently focusing on enhancing productivity and streamlining operations. From leading large-scale software engineering teams to driving advanced automation within enterprise systems, my approach has always involved deep dives into process optimization, robust standardization, and applying data-driven insights to achieve superior service levels and cost performance. I am adept at applying methodologies akin to Lean and Six Sigma within technology development lifecycles, ensuring continuous process improvement and the successful execution of complex projects. 

 I am particularly drawn to your emphasis on designing and implementing automation solutions, developing robust labor management strategies, and fostering cross-functional collaboration. My experience in architecting comprehensive technical roadmaps, conducting detailed ROI analysis, and mentoring high-performing teams will enable me to significantly contribute to your DC expansions, capital improvement projects, and the standardization of best practices across your rapidly growing distribution network. I am eager to leverage my leadership and problem-solving capabilities to drive Harbor Freight Tools' continued success and operational evolution. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi
