About the job

The Sr. Manager, Supply Chain & Labor Engineering leads engineering, process design, and labor management initiatives across a high-volume, rapidly growing distribution network. This role is responsible for designing, implementing, and standardizing operational processes, labor management systems, slotting strategies, and automation solutions to optimize capacity, productivity, efficiency, and service levels.

The role collaborates with DC operations leaders, corporate supply chain partners, and executive leadership to plan and execute capital improvement projects, DC expansions, operational strategies, automation implementations, and continuous process improvement initiatives. The ideal candidate is a strategic and analytical leader capable of influencing senior leadership, driving change, and fostering cross-functional collaboration. Lean, Six Sigma, continuous improvement expertise and thought process are required.

Duties And Responsibilities


Lead engineering and process teams in the design, approval, and execution of operational improvement, transition, and expansion projects. 
Lead the design and enhancement of new and existing DCs, including layout, material handling, asset recovery, and automation solutions.
Develop and govern DC Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), ensuring consistency across the network. 
Own labor management (LM) program strategy, including work standards, time studies, productivity tracking, and LM standardization across sites.
Develop and execute slotting strategies, tools, and protocols to optimize warehouse efficiency, store service, and inventory flow.
Partner with operations leaders to identify and implement continuous process improvements to drive service, safety, quality, and cost performance.
Lead cross-functional project teams across Operations, IT, Transportation, Inventory, Allocations, and Engineering to implement change initiatives.
Apply Lean, Six Sigma, and structured problem-solving methods to deliver rapid and sustainable operational improvements.
Conduct ROI analysis, business case development, and capital planning for projects and operational initiatives.
Standardize processes, share best practices, and drive collaboration across the distribution network.
Mentor, develop, and guide engineering, operations, LM, and slotting teams. 
Validate project milestones and performance outcomes, ensuring high-quality, on-time delivery.
Travel up to 35–50% to support network operations, project rollouts, and stakeholder engagement 


Scope


Staff supervision and development: Yes
Decision making: 
Travel: Up to 50%
Flex Designation: Anywhere


The anticipated salary range for this position is $136,900 – $205,200 depending on location, knowledge, skills, education and experience. This position is also eligible for an annual discretionary bonus. In addition, we offer comprehensive and competitive benefits to Associates (and their families) such as medical, dental, vision, life insurance, short-term and long-term disability. Eligible Associates are able to enroll in our company’s 401k plan, as well as a deferred compensation plan. Associates will accrue paid time off up to 236 hours per year, (inclusive of PTO, floating holidays and paid holidays). Paid sick time up to 80 hours per year unless otherwise required by law.

RequirementsEducation And Experience


Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering
Master’s Degree in Business, Engineering or Operations Management a plus
8+ years of experience in high volume distribution preferred


Skills


Education and/or Experience - Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering
Master’s Degree in Business, Engineering or Operations Management a plus
8+ years of experience in high volume distribution preferred
Strong leadership, influencing, and cross-functional collaboration skills.
Expertise in labor management, time studies, productivity tracking, process engineering, and slotting.
Proficient in project management, automation design, capital planning, and operational improvement implementation.
Experience applying Lean, Six Sigma, or other continuous improvement methodologies.
Exceptional analytical, financial, and problem-solving capabilities.
Outstanding communication skills across all levels of management.
Ability to manage multiple complex projects simultaneously with attention to detail.
Time & Motion, MTM, MSD, or MOST experience preferred.
Skilled in developing and standardizing operational processes
Experience leading operational transitions, DC design, and automation initiatives.
Ability to mentor and develop engineering, operations, and labor management teams.
Proven track record driving continuous improvement, operational efficiency, and productivity gains.
Competencies: Initiative, planning & organization, problem-solving, policy compliance, and leadership aligned with company values.
Competencies:
Leadership - Models the Company’s values and influences others to achieve individual or team objectives
Communication - Clearly conveys information through a variety of media to individuals or groups in a manner that is understandable, easy to retain and timely
Initiative - Approaches work objectives with a sense of urgency and a dedication to exceeding performance expectations
Problem-solving/Decision-making - Accurately identifies problems, issues and opportunities, collects data to draw conclusions and chooses a course of action based on facts
Planning & Organization - Establishes courses of action for self and others to ensure that work objectives are met
Policy Compliance - Manages in accordance with Company policies and procedures and promotes compliance in others
Physical Requirements

Corporate - Remote - General office environment requiring ability to: *Stand, walk, sit for extended periods of time . *Speak and listen to others in person and over the phone and video conferencing. *Use keyboard and read from computer screen and reports. *The ability to lift up to 15 lbs.

Safety

Must be able to perform this job safely in accordance with standard operating procedures and good manufacturing practices, without endangering the health or safety of self or others.

jobTitle: Director, Software Engineering (Conference Tech Platform Integration)
analysis: The summary and competencies have been thoroughly optimized to align with the 'Director, Software Engineering (Conference Tech Platform Integration)' job description at Gartner. Key requirements such as leading cross-regional teams, executing platform integration roadmaps, driving engineering excellence, strengthening SDLC rigor, and embedding responsible AI practices are explicitly woven throughout the summary and competencies. Specific technologies like AWS, API-centric, and event-driven platforms, alongside the emphasis on personalized user and attendee experiences through AI, are strongly highlighted. Irrelevant jargon ('clinical') has been removed. The cover letter is highly tailored, referencing past experiences (7-Eleven) and the MIT Agentic AI program to demonstrate a direct fit for Gartner's mission and AI focus. The structure and formatting for all sections adhere strictly to the specified rules, ensuring high ATS compatibility and executive-grade presentation.
summary: Visionary Engineering Leader with over 20 years of software experience, adept at building and scaling high-performing cross-regional engineering teams. Proven track record in executing strategic technical roadmaps for enterprise platform integrations, driving rapid agile development, and deploying secure, scalable, and AI-powered solutions. Expert in cultivating a quality-first culture, strengthening SDLC rigor, ensuring architectural governance, and leveraging cloud-native (AWS) API-centric and event-driven platforms. Dedicated to delivering seamless, personalized user and attendee experiences, embedding responsible AI practices, and leading organizational change to align technology with strategic business priorities for world-class outcomes.
coverLetter:
Rynnetta Garner and the Gartner Technology Hiring Team 

 Irving, TX 

 Subject: Application for Director, Software Engineering (Conference Tech Platform Integration) 

 Dear Rynnetta Garner and the Gartner Technology Hiring Team, 

 I am writing to express my enthusiastic interest in the Director, Software Engineering position for Conference Tech Platform Integration at Gartner. With over two decades of software engineering experience and a proven track record in leadership roles, I am drawn to Gartner's mission of guiding the leaders who shape the world and your commitment to nonstop innovation. My journey has centered on building and scaling high-performing engineering teams, executing strategic technical roadmaps, and delivering innovative digital solutions that drive business impact. 

 My experience, particularly in leading complex technology initiatives at companies like 7-Eleven, aligns seamlessly with the demands of leading cross-regional engineering teams to execute conference platform integrations. I have a strong execution background in enterprise platform integrations and cloud architecture, including extensive work with AWS and developing API-centric and event-driven platforms, which are critical for delivering the seamless, world-class experiences Gartner seeks for C-level executives. My tenure also includes fostering engineering excellence, strengthening SDLC rigor, and driving high-quality, scalable solutions in agile environments. 

 Furthermore, I am particularly excited by the opportunity to leverage AI-powered features within digital platforms to create personalized, intuitive user experiences and elevate attendee engagement. My recent immersion in the MIT Agentic AI program has deepened my understanding of how to ethically and effectively integrate cutting-edge AI capabilities to transform digital experiences and align with responsible AI practices. I am passionate about cultivating a quality-first culture, managing distributed teams, and collaborating across IT and business functions to drive organizational change and ensure the successful adoption of new platforms. 

 Gartner’s position at the center of the AI revolution, coupled with its culture of limitless growth and innovation, resonates deeply with my professional aspirations. I am confident that my strategic leadership, technical expertise, and passion for continuous improvement would make a significant contribution to your team and the impactful work you do. I look forward to the possibility of discussing how my experience can benefit Gartner. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

Job poster

Ask about job
About the job

Hiring near our Irving, TX and Stamford, CT Centers of Excellence with a flexible environment.

About Gartner IT:

Join a world-class team of skilled engineers who build creative digital solutions to support our colleagues and clients. We make a broad organizational impact by delivering cutting-edge technology solutions that power Gartner. Gartner IT values its culture of nonstop innovation, an outcome-driven approach to success, and the notion that great ideas can come from anyone on the team.

About the Role: 

We are seeking a Director of Software Engineering to lead the Conference Tech platform integrations, to enable a seamless, world-class experience for C-level executives. This role will be responsible for leading cross regional engineering teams to execute platform integrations, elevate engineering excellence, and deliver high-quality, scalable solutions aligned with business strategy.

What You’ll Do:


Lead Engineering: Lead a cross regional engineering team to deliver innovative solutions while fostering ownership, accountability, and high performance. 
Drive Platform Integration: Execute the conference platform integration roadmap, influence technology direction, and ensure successful delivery in an agile environment. 
Enable Engineering Excellence: Strengthen SDLC rigor, platform architecture, and engineering discipline, embedding responsible AI practices to deliver secure, scalable, and future ready enterprise solutions. 
Partner & Lead Change: Partner with Conference business and IT leaders to align technology solutions to strategic priorities, and lead change to ensure successful adoption of new platforms, processes, and ways of working. 
Champion Quality: Drive a quality first culture leveraging AI powered, responsible SDLC practices that improve reliability, scalability, and delivery efficiency. 


What You Will Need:


Education: Bachelor’s or master’s in computer science or related field. 
Experience: 12–15 years in software engineering, with at least 5 years in leadership roles. 
Technical Expertise: Strong execution background in enterprise platform integrations, cloud architecture (AWS), Object oriented programming – .NET/Java/Ruby, and expertise in building enterprise‑scale API‑centric and event driven platforms. Experience leveraging AI powered features within digital platforms to deliver personalized, intuitive user experiences and to improve user engagement and elevate attendee experiences. 
Leadership Skills: Proven ability to manage distributed teams and drive organizational change. Ability to collaborate across IT and business functions to drive innovation. 
Preferred: Curiosity and drive for continuous learning. Experience leading large platform integration initiatives. 


Who You Are:


A strategic leader dedicated to building and sustaining high performing teams equipped for the future. 
Collaborative and adaptable, thriving in a fast-paced, evolving environment. 
Passionate about technology, innovation, and continuous improvement. 
A trusted, influential communicator who fosters alignment and momentum through integrity, transparency, and clear engagement across technical and business stakeholders. 


Don’t meet every single requirement? We encourage you to apply anyway. You might just be the right candidate for this, or other roles!

What you will get:


Competitive compensation. 
Limitless growth and learning opportunities. 
Ongoing mentorship and apprenticeship; Leadership courses, development programs, technical courses, certification opportunities and more!
A collaborative and positive culture - join a diverse team of professionals that are as smart and driven as you. 
A chance to make an impact – your work will contribute directly to our strategy. 
Enjoy the flexibility of working from home and the energy of collaborating with peers in our dynamic offices. 
20+ PTO days plus holidays and floating holidays in your first year. 
Extensive medical, dental insurance and vision plan. 
401K with corporate match, immediate vesting. 
Health-and-wellness-related allowance programs. 
Parental leave. 
Tuition reimbursement. 
Employee Stock Purchase Plan. 
Employee Assistance Program. 
Gartner Gives Charity Match. And much more!


Who are we? 

At Gartner, Inc. (NYSE:IT), we guide the leaders who shape the world.

Our mission relies on expert analysis and bold ideas to deliver actionable, objective business and technology insights, helping enterprise leaders and their teams succeed with their mission-critical priorities.

Since our founding in 1979, we’ve grown to 20,000 associates globally who support over 13,000 client enterprises in ~90 countries and territories. We do important, interesting and substantive work that matters. That’s why we hire associates with the intellectual curiosity, energy and drive to want to make a difference. The bar is unapologetically high. So is the impact you can have here.

What makes Gartner a great place to work? 

Our vast, virtually untapped market potential offers limitless opportunities – opportunities that may not even exist right now – for you to grow professionally and flourish personally. How far you go is driven by your passion and performance.

We hire remarkable people who collaborate and win as a team. Together, our singular, unifying goal is to deliver results for our clients.

Our teams are inclusive and composed of individuals from different geographies, cultures, religions, ethnicities, races, genders, sexual orientations, abilities and generations.

We invest in great leaders who bring out the best in you and the company, enabling us to multiply our impact and results. This is why, year after year, we are recognized worldwide as a great place to work.

Gartner is the world authority on AI

At Gartner, you’ll join a company at the very center of the AI revolution. Gartner has proactive, objective guidance throughout clients’ AI journeys. We set the standard for how organizations leverage artificial intelligence to drive meaningful impact. You’ll have access to unmatched resources, expertise, and technology, and play a key role in helping Gartner and our clients innovate and grow as we leverage AI to transform business and technology landscapes.

It’s an exciting time to be at Gartner, with limitless opportunities to make a real impact, grow your skills, and build a lasting, meaningful career in a field that’s reshaping the way we operate. If you’re passionate about AI and want to be part of a team that’s guiding the leaders who shape the world, Gartner is the place for you.

What do we offer? 

Gartner offers world-class benefits, highly competitive compensation and disproportionate rewards for top performers.

In our hybrid work environment, we provide the flexibility and support for you to thrive — working virtually when it's productive to do so and getting together with colleagues in a vibrant community that is purposeful, engaging and inspiring.

Ready to grow your career with Gartner? Join us.

Gartner believes in fair and equitable pay. A reasonable estimate of the base salary range for this role is 128,000 USD - 189,000 USD. Please note that actual salaries may vary within the range, or be above or below the range, based on factors including, but not limited to, education, training, experience, professional achievement, business need, and location. In addition to base salary, employees will participate in either an annual bonus plan based on company and individual performance, or a role-based, uncapped sales incentive plan. Our talent acquisition team will provide the specific opportunity on our bonus or incentive programs to eligible candidates. We also offer market leading benefit programs including generous PTO, a 401k match up to $7,200 per year, the opportunity to purchase company stock at a discount, and more.

The policy of Gartner is to provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, citizenship status, age, national origin, ancestry, disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected status and to seek to advance the principles of equal employment opportunity.

Gartner is committed to being an Equal Opportunity Employer and offers opportunities to all job seekers, including job seekers with disabilities. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access the Company’s career webpage as a result of your disability. You may request reasonable accommodations by calling Human Resources at +1 (203) 964-0096 or by sending an email to ApplicantAccommodations@gartner.com.

Job Requisition ID:110248

By submitting your information and application, you confirm that you have read and agree to the country or regional recruitment notice linked below applicable to your place of residence.

Gartner Applicant Privacy Link: https://jobs.gartner.com/applicant-privacy-policy

For efficient navigation through the application, please only use the back button within the application, not the back arrow within your browser.

We deliver actionable, objective business and technology insights. Our expert guidance and tools enable faster, smarter decisions and stronger performance on an organization’s mission-critical priorities.

Our unrivaled combination of business and technology insights steers clients toward the right decisions on the issues that matter most. As a $6.5 billion company, with over 20,000 associates, and a member of the S&P 500, we are an objective partner with 13,000+ client enterprises in ~90 countries and territories — across every major function, geography, industry and market sector — and boast 45+ years of experience providing insights and expert guidance to client enterprises worldwide.

Commitments

Career growth and learning

Talent development is foundational to driving associate engagement and essential to Gartner’s continued market leadership. As our ability to grow and adapt as an organization requires that we help our associates reach their full potential, continuous improvement is key to our talent development strategy. Through ongoing feedback and goal setting, we help our associates understand their top priorities and how those priorities result in value for Gartner clients. Every associate is encouraged to create an individual development plan, which includes career interests and areas of strengths and development needs.
Connecting their work to Gartner’s business goals helps our associates develop engagement and purpose. We see it as a core success factor that enabled over 4,700 of our associates — or over 24% of our workforce — to earn promotions in 2022. What’s more, we filled 25% of our open positions with internal candidates.
… more

Work-life balance

Keeping connected in a hybrid virtual-first environment
We come to work each day, whether at the office or at home, to serve our clients, both internal and external, and to connect with colleagues. Gartner operates under a hybrid virtual-first working arrangement, which is perfectly suited to our digital business, as the majority of our products and services are digital. This purposeful strategy allows flexibility while fostering connection and collaboration:
Most associates can work remotely the majority of the time and have the ability to use our world-class offices for team meetings, other forms of in-person collaboration and uninterrupted focus. Our approach is suited to meet the needs and expectations of a changing workforce. Moreover, it allows associates to join
forces and collaborate on a global scale, eliminating the boundaries of physical distance. To ensure that everybody remains connected, we have invested in collaboration technologies and have brought associates together through in-person team meetings, ERG learning opportunities and events and other collaboration opportunities at the office.

summary: A highly accomplished Engineering Leader with over two decades of software development experience, including more than 7 years dedicated to native Android development. Proven expertise in architecting and delivering robust, scalable mobile applications, adeptly navigating the Android SDK across diverse versions and screen sizes. Specializes in seamless RESTful API integration, optimizing performance through efficient offline storage and threading, and ensuring exceptional user experiences via intuitive Android UI design principles, patterns, and best practices. Committed to fostering technical excellence and continuous improvement through rigorous code quality automation and CI/CD pipeline integration, translating intricate business requirements into precise technical specifications.
competencies: \item \textbf{Native Android Development Expertise:} Proficient in Android SDK across various versions, designing applications for diverse screen sizes, and leveraging the open-source Android ecosystem and its libraries for common tasks.\item \textbf{RESTful API Integration and Backend Services:} Strong experience connecting Android applications to robust back-end services, ensuring seamless data flow and efficient communication via RESTful APIs.\item \textbf{Android UI/UX Design Principles:} Deep knowledge of Android UI design principles, patterns, and best practices, crafting intuitive and natural user interfaces for optimal engagement.\item \textbf{Application Performance and Optimization:} Expert in optimizing Android application performance, implementing efficient offline storage solutions, and managing threading for a responsive user experience.\item \textbf{Code Quality and Automation (CI/CD):} Dedicated to automating code quality, performance testing, and unit testing within CI/CD pipelines, utilizing tools like SonarQube and Findbugs for continuous improvement.\item \textbf{Technical Requirements Translation:} Skilled at understanding complex business requirements and meticulously translating them into clear, actionable technical specifications and architectural designs.\item \textbf{Sensor Integration and Device Capabilities:} Familiarity with integrating additional device sensors, such as gyroscopes and accelerometers, to create enriched application functionalities.\item \textbf{Build Processing and Deployment Excellence:} Expert in streamlining build processing, implementing automated unit testing, and ensuring robust CI/CD pipeline integrity for rapid, high-quality application deployment.
coverLetter:
Vinu Rao and the Photon Hiring Team 

 Irving, TX 

 Subject: Application for Sr Android Developer 

 Dear Vinu Rao and the Photon Hiring Team, 

 It is with genuine enthusiasm that I submit my application for the Sr Android Developer position at Photon in Irving, TX. With over two decades of dedicated experience in software engineering and a proven history of delivering impactful, scalable solutions for global enterprises like 7-Eleven and CVS Health, I am eager to contribute my hands-on technical expertise and passion for mobile innovation to your team. My career journey has been defined by a commitment to building robust, high-performance applications and a relentless pursuit of engineering excellence. 

 Throughout my extensive career, I have consistently driven the development of complex, user-centric Android platforms, demonstrating strong proficiency in native Android development, architecting intuitive UI designs, and integrating seamlessly with diverse back-end services via RESTful APIs. My background encompasses optimizing application performance, implementing efficient offline storage and threading, and ensuring robust code quality through automated testing and CI/CD pipelines, aligning precisely with the responsibilities outlined in your job description. 

 While my recent journey has included advanced work in Agentic AI at MIT, this experience has deepened my analytical capabilities and reinforced my commitment to continuous learning and cutting-edge problem-solving, skills I believe are invaluable to any senior development role. I am excited by Photon's leadership in AI and digital transformation and see this role as an opportunity to apply my foundational software engineering strengths to contribute directly to your next generation of digital platforms. I am confident my blend of extensive development experience and strategic perspective would make a significant, immediate impact at Photon. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

coverLetter:
Robert Franwood and the Ethos Hiring Team 

 United States 

 Subject: Application for IT Director / Head of Engineering 

 Dear Robert Franwood and the Ethos Hiring Team, 

 I am writing to express my profound interest in the IT Director / Head of Engineering opportunity at Ethos. Your mission to leverage cutting-edge AI to match experts with opportunities and advance economic possibilities deeply resonates with my journey as an engineering leader. With over two decades of experience in software development and a specialized focus on building and scaling AI/ML teams, I am particularly drawn to the challenge of training your latest language model on complex IT management and systems leadership tasks. 

 My background includes architecting high-scale enterprise systems at organizations like 7-Eleven and driving significant technological advancements in dynamic environments. I have consistently led teams in executing strategic technology roadmaps, defining robust systems architecture, and establishing comprehensive IT governance frameworks, all of which are directly relevant to the core workflows mentioned in this role. Furthermore, my recent completion of the MIT Agentic AI program has equipped me with advanced insights into leveraging LLM applications for sophisticated, multi-step reasoning and refining AI-generated work. 

 I thrive on translating executive business objectives into actionable engineering strategies and possess a proven ability to lead, evaluate, and refine AI outputs with meticulous attention to detail and excellent written communication. My experience in developing production-grade agentic workflows and leading technology teams from concept to deployment makes me uniquely suited to contribute to your innovative approach. The flexible, remote nature of this role aligns perfectly with my preferred working style, allowing me to contribute effectively while maintaining autonomy. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Candescent 

 Atlanta, GA 

 Subject: Application for Director of Engineering, Mobile Platform 

 Dear Hiring Team Candescent, 

 I am writing to express my profound interest in the Director of Engineering, Mobile Platform role at Candescent. With over two decades of engineering leadership experience, I am deeply drawn to Candescent's mission of transforming digital banking through intelligent, cloud-powered solutions. My career journey has been dedicated to building and scaling robust platforms that connect diverse customer experiences, drive innovation, and deliver consistent, reliable results, aligning perfectly with your vision for the application foundation underpinning consumer, business, and commercial banking. 

 My tenure leading significant technology initiatives at organizations like 7-Eleven involved architecting and scaling high-performance, customer-facing platforms, ensuring exceptional reliability and a seamless developer experience. I bring extensive expertise in owning the full API lifecycle, leading gateway engineering, and defining comprehensive observability practices—all crucial for environments where the platform truly is the product. My background includes successfully guiding globally distributed teams of 50-70 employees, fostering a culture of technical excellence and continuous improvement while accelerating delivery cadences and maintaining quality. 

 I am particularly excited by the opportunity to guide the cross-platform product expansion and lead the transition to a modular, plugin-driven architecture. My strategic approach to platform evolution, including a focus on internal developer experience, SDK design, and self-service tooling, has consistently enabled product teams to build independently and ship faster. My recent engagement with advanced architectural principles, including those explored at MIT, further deepens my strategic approach to evolving digital platforms and anticipating future demands within dynamic sectors like FinTech. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

About the job

Candescent is the leading cloud-based digital banking solutions provider for financial institutions. We are transforming digital banking with intelligent, cloud-powered solutions that connect account opening, digital banking, and branch experiences for financial institutions. Our advanced technology and developer tools enable seamless, differentiated customer journeys that elevate trust, service, and innovation. Success here requires flexibility in a fast-paced environment, a client-first mindset, and a commitment to delivering consistent, reliable results as part of a performance-driven, values-led team. With team members around the world, Candescent is an equal opportunity employer.

Director of Engineering, Mobile Platform

You thrive in environments where the platform is the product where reliability, developer experience, and extensibility are core features. You’re comfortable navigating build‑vs‑buy decisions, managing technical debt alongside new capabilities, and communicating tradeoffs clearly to executive leadership.

The Opportunity

Candescent is seeking a Director of Engineering, Mobile Platform to lead the evolution of the application foundation that powers our consumer digital banking experience today and will expand into business and commercial banking. This is not a narrow mobile role: you will own the cross‑cutting platform layer that underpins both mobile and web applications.

You will guide the architecture, reliability, and developer experience for the application foundation covering instrumentation, authentication, observability, error handling, logging, telemetry, and plugin infrastructure that product teams rely on to build and ship features.

Key Areas of Responsibility

Platform Architecture & Extensibility


Design and evolve extensibility surfaces including public/internal APIs, event-driven interfaces, webhooks, and plugin endpoints that enable integrations and custom applications.
Develop resilient, production-grade services using modern service frameworks and proven reliability patterns (e.g., retries, circuit breaking, graceful degradation).
Own the full API lifecycle: requirements, design, implementation, versioning, backward compatibility, and deprecation strategy.
Lead gateway engineering: authentication/authorization policies, rate limiting, traffic shaping, caching, and progressive deployment strategies.

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Candescent 

 Atlanta, GA 

 Subject: Application for Director of Engineering, Mobile Platform 

 Dear Hiring Team Candescent, 

 My career journey of over two decades in software development and engineering leadership has been defined by a passion for building and scaling high-performing technical organizations and robust, production-grade platforms. The opportunity at Candescent, leading the evolution of your Mobile Platform and transforming digital banking with intelligent, cloud-powered solutions, deeply resonates with my expertise in architecting critical, customer-facing systems and fostering developer excellence. I am particularly drawn to Candescent's mission of unifying data and channels to deliver differentiated experiences and accelerate growth for financial institutions. 

 Throughout my tenure, including leadership roles at companies like 7-Eleven, I have consistently driven the design, implementation, and scaling of complex application foundations. My experience encompasses full API lifecycle management, establishing comprehensive observability practices, and evolving authentication and identity frameworks that support diverse user segments, all critical requirements for this Director role. I thrive in environments where the platform is the product, and I am adept at leading teams to deliver resilient, extensible services, ensuring exceptional reliability and developer experience. 

 I have a proven track record of leading and scaling globally distributed engineering organizations, encompassing 50-70 employees across diverse geographies. My leadership philosophy centers on cultivating a culture of ownership, technical excellence, and continuous improvement, optimizing development workflows and delivery cadences for high-growth environments. I am confident that my strategic vision and hands-on experience in platform engineering, coupled with a client-first mindset, will enable me to significantly contribute to Candescent's expansion from consumer into business and commercial banking, ensuring a scalable and secure application foundation. 

 Thank you for considering my application. I am enthusiastic about the prospect of bringing my leadership experience and dedication to innovation to Candescent and contributing to your impactful mission in digital banking. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

About the job

Candescent is the leading cloud-based digital banking solutions provider for financial institutions. We are transforming digital banking with intelligent, cloud-powered solutions that connect account opening, digital banking, and branch experiences for financial institutions. Our advanced technology and developer tools enable seamless, differentiated customer journeys that elevate trust, service, and innovation. Success here requires flexibility in a fast-paced environment, a client-first mindset, and a commitment to delivering consistent, reliable results as part of a performance-driven, values-led team. With team members around the world, Candescent is an equal opportunity employer.

Director of Engineering, Mobile Platform

You thrive in environments where the platform is the product where reliability, developer experience, and extensibility are core features. You’re comfortable navigating build‑vs‑buy decisions, managing technical debt alongside new capabilities, and communicating tradeoffs clearly to executive leadership.

The Opportunity

Candescent is seeking a Director of Engineering, Mobile Platform to lead the evolution of the application foundation that powers our consumer digital banking experience today and will expand into business and commercial banking. This is not a narrow mobile role: you will own the cross‑cutting platform layer that underpins both mobile and web applications.

You will guide the architecture, reliability, and developer experience for the application foundation covering instrumentation, authentication, observability, error handling, logging, telemetry, and plugin infrastructure that product teams rely on to build and ship features.

Key Areas of Responsibility

Platform Architecture & Extensibility


Design and evolve extensibility surfaces including public/internal APIs, event-driven interfaces, webhooks, and plugin endpoints that enable integrations and custom applications.
Develop resilient, production-grade services using modern service frameworks and proven reliability patterns (e.g., retries, circuit breaking, graceful degradation).
Own the full API lifecycle: requirements, design, implementation, versioning, backward compatibility, and deprecation strategy.
Lead gateway engineering: authentication/authorization policies, rate limiting, traffic shaping, caching, and progressive deployment strategies.


Reliability, Observability & Performance


Define and enforce performance and reliability standards across services and APIs, including latency, throughput, error budgets, and SLA/SLO adherence.
Integrate performance and reliability testing into CI/CD workflows.
Establish comprehensive observability practices: structured logging, metrics, distributed tracing, and real-time monitoring.
Drive proactive incident prevention and root-cause analysis through data and instrumentation.


Application Foundation & Developer Experience


Own instrumentation, error handling, logging, and telemetry across mobile and web surfaces.
Lead the evolution of authentication and identity frameworks supporting consumer, business, and future commercial banking users.
Build and maintain the observability stack and performance analytics for the application layer.
Develop plugin architecture and scaffolding that enables product teams to build independently and ship faster.
Deliver high-quality developer documentation, API collections, sandbox environments, and SDKs that reduce time-to-first-call.


Cross-Platform Product Expansion


Define the platform strategy as Candescent expands from consumer into business and commercial banking.
Ensure the application foundation supports both native mobile and web delivery models.
Partner with Product, Design, and peer engineering leaders to define the technical roadmap and API contracts.
Lead the transition from monolithic patterns to modular, plugin-driven architecture.


Global Team Leadership & Scaling


Lead team and engineering managers and their teams across North America, India, and Serbia. Team size 50-70 employees.
Scale the organization while maintaining velocity, quality, and a strong engineering culture.
Establish engineering standards, development workflows, and delivery cadences optimized for distributed teams.
Foster a culture of ownership, technical excellence, and continuous improvement.


Required Experience


Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or equivalent work experience.
8+ years of hands-on experience building and operating production services using modern service frameworks.
Experience with mobile and/or web application development from 0→1, with strong architectural instincts.
Deep expertise in API design, lifecycle management, and platform engineering at scale.
Experience working with full stack and React Native.
Proven success leading and scaling globally distributed engineering organizations.
Strong understanding of authentication/authorization protocols and security best practices.
Experience operating API gateways and managing traffic and security policies.
Demonstrated ability to establish observability practices and drive reliability improvements.
Must be legally authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in digital banking, payments, lending, or other regulated FinTech domains.
Background in platform-as-a-product models, including internal developer experience, SDK design, and self-service tooling.
Experience in PE-backed or high-growth environments undergoing rapid transformation.


Statement to Third Party Agencies

To ALL recruitment agencies: Candescent only accepts resumes from agencies on the preferred supplier list. Please do not forward resumes to our applicant tracking system, Candescent employees, or any Candescent facility. Candescent is not responsible for any fees or charges associated with unsolicited resumes.

Own instrumentation, error handling, logging, and telemetry across mobile and web surfaces.
Lead the evolution of authentication and identity frameworks supporting consumer, business, and future commercial banking users.
Build and maintain the observability stack and performance analytics for the application layer.
Develop plugin architecture and scaffolding that enables product teams to build independently and ship faster.
Deliver high-quality developer documentation, API collections, sandbox environments, and SDKs that reduce time-to-first-call.


Cross-Platform Product Expansion


Define the platform strategy as Candescent expands from consumer into business and commercial banking.
Ensure the application foundation supports both native mobile and web delivery models.
Partner with Product, Design, and peer engineering leaders to define the technical roadmap and API contracts.
Lead the transition from monolithic patterns to modular, plugin-driven architecture.


Global Team Leadership & Scaling


Lead team and engineering managers and their teams across North America, India, and Serbia. Team size 50-70 employees.
Scale the organization while maintaining velocity, quality, and a strong engineering culture.
Establish engineering standards, development workflows, and delivery cadences optimized for distributed teams.
Foster a culture of ownership, technical excellence, and continuous improvement.


Required Experience


Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or equivalent work experience.
8+ years of hands-on experience building and operating production services using modern service frameworks.
Experience with mobile and/or web application development from 0→1, with strong architectural instincts.
Deep expertise in API design, lifecycle management, and platform engineering at scale.
Experience working with full stack and React Native.
Proven success leading and scaling globally distributed engineering organizations.
Strong understanding of authentication/authorization protocols and security best practices.
Experience operating API gateways and managing traffic and security policies.
Demonstrated ability to establish observability practices and drive reliability improvements.
Must be legally authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in digital banking, payments, lending, or other regulated FinTech domains.
Background in platform-as-a-product models, including internal developer experience, SDK design, and self-service tooling.
Experience in PE-backed or high-growth environments undergoing rapid transformation.

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Candescent 

 Atlanta, GA 

 Subject: Application for Director of Engineering, Mobile Platform 

 Dear Hiring Team Candescent, 

 I am writing to express my profound interest in the Director of Engineering, Mobile Platform role at Candescent. With over 20 years of experience leading engineering teams to build and scale robust, customer-centric digital platforms, I am deeply aligned with Candescent's mission to transform digital banking. I thrive in environments where the platform is truly the product, and I am adept at navigating the complexities of architectural evolution, ensuring reliability, and fostering an exceptional developer experience. 

 My journey in leadership, particularly at organizations like 7-Eleven and CVS Health, has equipped me with invaluable experience in architecting and operating high-performance, trusted systems crucial for large-scale consumer-facing services. I bring a strong background in API design, lifecycle management, establishing comprehensive observability practices, and evolving authentication and identity frameworks. My expertise extends to both mobile and web application development, including full stack and React Native, and I am passionate about driving the transition from monolithic patterns to modular, plugin-driven architectures to enable product teams to ship faster. 

 I am particularly drawn to the opportunity to lead the evolution of your application foundation and expand Candescent's reach into business and commercial banking. My proven ability to lead and scale globally distributed engineering organizations of 50-70 employees, define technical roadmaps, and instill a culture of technical excellence and continuous improvement aligns perfectly with the requirements outlined. I am confident in my capacity to partner with Product and Design leaders to deliver consistent, reliable results and propel Candescent's continued growth. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

About the job

Candescent is the leading cloud-based digital banking solutions provider for financial institutions. We are transforming digital banking with intelligent, cloud-powered solutions that connect account opening, digital banking, and branch experiences for financial institutions. Our advanced technology and developer tools enable seamless, differentiated customer journeys that elevate trust, service, and innovation. Success here requires flexibility in a fast-paced environment, a client-first mindset, and a commitment to delivering consistent, reliable results as part of a performance-driven, values-led team. With team members around the world, Candescent is an equal opportunity employer.

Director of Engineering, Mobile Platform

You thrive in environments where the platform is the product where reliability, developer experience, and extensibility are core features. You’re comfortable navigating build‑vs‑buy decisions, managing technical debt alongside new capabilities, and communicating tradeoffs clearly to executive leadership.

The Opportunity

Candescent is seeking a Director of Engineering, Mobile Platform to lead the evolution of the application foundation that powers our consumer digital banking experience today and will expand into business and commercial banking. This is not a narrow mobile role: you will own the cross‑cutting platform layer that underpins both mobile and web applications.

You will guide the architecture, reliability, and developer experience for the application foundation covering instrumentation, authentication, observability, error handling, logging, telemetry, and plugin infrastructure that product teams rely on to build and ship features.

Key Areas of Responsibility

Platform Architecture & Extensibility


Design and evolve extensibility surfaces including public/internal APIs, event-driven interfaces, webhooks, and plugin endpoints that enable integrations and custom applications.
Develop resilient, production-grade services using modern service frameworks and proven reliability patterns (e.g., retries, circuit breaking, graceful degradation).
Own the full API lifecycle: requirements, design, implementation, versioning, backward compatibility, and deprecation strategy.
Lead gateway engineering: authentication/authorization policies, rate limiting, traffic shaping, caching, and progressive deployment strategies.


Reliability, Observability & Performance


Define and enforce performance and reliability standards across services and APIs, including latency, throughput, error budgets, and SLA/SLO adherence.
Integrate performance and reliability testing into CI/CD workflows.
Establish comprehensive observability practices: structured logging, metrics, distributed tracing, and real-time monitoring.
Drive proactive incident prevention and root-cause analysis through data and instrumentation.


Application Foundation & Developer Experience


Own instrumentation, error handling, logging, and telemetry across mobile and web surfaces.
Lead the evolution of authentication and identity frameworks supporting consumer, business, and future commercial banking users.
Build and maintain the observability stack and performance analytics for the application layer.
Develop plugin architecture and scaffolding that enables product teams to build independently and ship faster.
Deliver high-quality developer documentation, API collections, sandbox environments, and SDKs that reduce time-to-first-call.


Cross-Platform Product Expansion


Define the platform strategy as Candescent expands from consumer into business and commercial banking.
Ensure the application foundation supports both native mobile and web delivery models.
Partner with Product, Design, and peer engineering leaders to define the technical roadmap and API contracts.
Lead the transition from monolithic patterns to modular, plugin-driven architecture.


Global Team Leadership & Scaling


Lead team and engineering managers and their teams across North America, India, and Serbia. Team size 50-70 employees.
Scale the organization while maintaining velocity, quality, and a strong engineering culture.
Establish engineering standards, development workflows, and delivery cadences optimized for distributed teams.
Foster a culture of ownership, technical excellence, and continuous improvement.


Required Experience


Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or equivalent work experience.
8+ years of hands-on experience building and operating production services using modern service frameworks.
Experience with mobile and/or web application development from 0→1, with strong architectural instincts.
Deep expertise in API design, lifecycle management, and platform engineering at scale.
Experience working with full stack and React Native.
Proven success leading and scaling globally distributed engineering organizations.
Strong understanding of authentication/authorization protocols and security best practices.
Experience operating API gateways and managing traffic and security policies.
Demonstrated ability to establish observability practices and drive reliability improvements.
Must be legally authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in digital banking, payments, lending, or other regulated FinTech domains.
Background in platform-as-a-product models, including internal developer experience, SDK design, and self-service tooling.
Experience in PE-backed or high-growth environments undergoing rapid transformation.


Statement to Third Party Agencies

To ALL recruitment agencies: Candescent only accepts resumes from agencies on the preferred supplier list. Please do not forward resumes to our applicant tracking system, Candescent employees, or any Candescent facility. Candescent is not responsible for any fees or charges associated with unsolicited resumes.

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Hello Heart 

 United States 

 Subject: Application for Director, Technical Operations 

 Dear Hiring Team Hello Heart, 

 I am writing to express my profound enthusiasm for the Director, Technical Operations position at Hello Heart. Your mission to make heart attacks a thing of the past by leveraging AI to predict and prevent cardiac events deeply resonates with my journey as an engineering leader focused on impactful, scalable solutions. With over two decades of experience building and scaling high-performing technical teams and executing complex strategic roadmaps, I am confident in my ability to drive the operational rigor and cross-functional leadership essential for delivering high-quality connected health products at scale. 

 The job description's emphasis on 'AI fluency' and the ability to 'build no-code AI workflows, automations, or AI agents to improve operational execution' immediately caught my attention. My recent completion of the MIT Agentic AI program and hands-on experience deploying production-grade agentic workflows and LLM applications position me uniquely to leverage AI to streamline your program management, automate repetitive tasks, and accelerate execution. My background at organizations like 7-Eleven and leadership roles within CVS Health have provided me with invaluable experience in managing large-scale, distributed systems and navigating the complexities of regulated environments, directly transferable to the demands of Hello Heart. 

 Throughout my career, I have consistently driven cross-functional alignment across product, engineering, and operations, owning program schedules, mitigating risks, and establishing scalable processes that enhance overall operational efficiency. My customer-centric focus, coupled with a commitment to clinical operational excellence, aligns seamlessly with Hello Heart’s pioneering work in preventive heart health. I thrive in fast-moving environments and am adept at balancing strategic thinking with detailed execution to ensure programs stay on track from concept through mass production. 

 I am eager to bring my unique blend of technical leadership, AI expertise, and operational acumen to Hello Heart, contributing to your groundbreaking efforts in defining how heart disease is managed in the AI era. Thank you for your time and consideration; I look forward to discussing this opportunity further. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

About the job

About Hello Heart

Hello Heart is on a mission to make heart attacks a thing of the past.

We’re an AI company focused exclusively on heart health, building a platform that predicts and prevents cardiac events before they happen—identifying risk up to 10 days in advance versus 10 years in traditional clinical models.

This is already working at scale. Hello Heart has been shown to reduce inpatient hospital days by 47% and deliver ~$1,800 in annual savings per member. Hello Heart is the cardiac prevention partner to over 80% of large U.S. health plans and serves hundreds of public and private employers.

We’re defining how the #1 cause of death—heart disease—is managed in the AI era. Join us.

About The Role

Hello Heart is looking for an experienced Director Technical Operations to lead the execution of hardware programs from early concept through mass production. Reporting into our Senior Director, Product and Manufacturing Operations, you will partner across Product, Engineering, Operations, Quality, and Supply Chain to drive alignment, manage complexity, and deliver high-quality connected health products at scale.

This role is ideal for someone who combines deep hardware development experience with exceptional cross-functional leadership and operational rigor. You should thrive in fast-moving environments, proactively identify risks, and use AI fluently to improve execution, automate workflows, and increase team efficiency.

Responsibilities


Lead end-to-end hardware program execution from feasibility through mass production
Build and maintain integrated program plans across hardware, firmware, validation, manufacturing, quality, and supply chain functions
Drive cross-functional alignment across Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Firmware, Operations, Quality, and external manufacturing partners
Own program schedules, milestones, dependencies, and risk management throughout the full product lifecycle
Coordinate EVT, DVT, PVT, and production readiness activities to ensure programs stay on track
Identify risks early, drive decision-making, and resolve blockers to maintain execution momentum
Partner with contract manufacturers and suppliers to support build readiness, production validation, and manufacturing ramp
Establish scalable processes, communication cadences, and execution frameworks that improve operational efficiency
Use AI tools proactively to streamline program management workflows, automate repetitive tasks, improve reporting, and accelerate execution
Build and maintain no-code AI workflows, automations, or agents that improve productivity, planning accuracy, risk tracking, documentation, and team coordination



Qualifications


Strong AI fluency with demonstrated ability building no-code AI workflows, automations, or AI agents that improve operational execution and team effectiveness
10+ years of experience in hardware product development, technical program management, or related technical operations roles
Proven track record of delivering physical products from concept through mass production
Deep understanding of EVT, DVT, PVT, manufacturing validation, and production ramp processes
Strong technical background across hardware systems, firmware integration, manufacturing workflows, and supply chain operations
Experience with PCB and PCBA development, validation testing, tooling, packaging, and factory processes
Experience working with contract manufacturers, suppliers, and globally distributed cross-functional teams
Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and organizational leadership skills
Ability to balance strategic thinking with detailed execution in a fast-paced environment
Experience within medical device, digital health, consumer electronics, or other regulated hardware environments is preferred
Ability to travel internationally (approximately 20~30%)



The US base salary range for this full-time position is $170,000 to $195,000. Salary ranges are determined by role and level. Compensation is determined by additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Please note that the compensation details listed in US role postings reflect the salary and bonus only, and do not include equity or benefits.

coverLetter:
Andrew Phelan and the Moonshot Labs Hiring Team 

 Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex, TX 

 Subject: Application for AI Development Manager 

 Dear Andrew Phelan and the Moonshot Labs Hiring Team, 

 It is with immense enthusiasm that I submit my application for the AI Development Manager position at Moonshot Labs. Having closely followed your innovative approach to turning AI inward to accelerate manufacturing workflows, I am deeply inspired by your mission to build practical, production-ready AI agents. My journey, encompassing over two decades in software engineering and executive leadership at companies like 7-Eleven and CVS Health, alongside my recent immersion in the MIT Agentic AI program, has uniquely prepared me to contribute to your vision. 

 At 7-Eleven, I spearheaded initiatives that scaled high-performance engineering teams and delivered robust backend architectures for enterprise-level systems, navigating complex roadmaps and rapid priority shifts. This experience cultivated my capabilities as a player-coach, making definitive architecture calls and setting a high technical bar, while also serving as a single point of contact for diverse stakeholders across Operations and Finance. My deep expertise in distributed systems and Agile/Scrum methodologies, coupled with a commitment to CI/CD pipelines and staging disciplines, aligns perfectly with the operational excellence Moonshot Labs embodies. 

 My recent engagement with the MIT Agentic AI program further solidifies my hands-on capability and interest in applied AI, LLMs, function calling, and agentic workflows. I am eager to leverage this specialized knowledge to build, deploy, and scale the very production-ready AI agents that will transform manufacturing at Moonshot Labs. The prospect of leading your software development team to accelerate workflows from the shop floor to leadership is genuinely exciting, and I am confident in my ability to drive impactful results. 

 Thank you for considering my application. I am eager to discuss how my leadership experience, technical acumen, and passion for practical AI innovation can significantly benefit Moonshot Labs. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Candescent 

 Atlanta, GA 

 Subject: Application for Director Engineering (Mobile Platform) 

 Dear Hiring Team Candescent, 

 I am writing to express my profound interest in the Director Engineering (Mobile Platform) position at Candescent. With over two decades of experience in software engineering leadership, I have dedicated my career journey to building and scaling high-performing platforms that underpin critical, customer-facing digital experiences, a mission that perfectly aligns with Candescent's transformative work in digital banking. I am particularly drawn to this opportunity to evolve an application foundation that is truly the product, where reliability, developer experience, and extensibility are paramount. 

 Throughout my leadership roles, particularly at organizations like 7-Eleven and CVS Health, I have spearheaded the development and operationalization of large-scale, complex platforms, managing extensive API lifecycles, and ensuring robust authentication and observability for millions of users. My background includes guiding architectural evolution from monolithic patterns to modular, plugin-driven systems, enhancing developer productivity with comprehensive documentation and SDKs, and securing enterprise-grade applications in highly regulated environments. This experience directly maps to your requirements for designing resilient services and leading the cross-cutting platform layer for mobile and web applications. 

 Furthermore, I bring a demonstrated ability to lead and scale globally distributed engineering organizations, fostering a culture of technical excellence and continuous improvement. Having successfully managed teams of 50-70 employees across various international locations, I am adept at establishing effective engineering standards and delivery cadences essential for Candescent's continued growth and expansion from consumer into business and commercial banking. My commitment to a client-first mindset and delivering consistent, reliable results aligns seamlessly with your values-led team. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

About the job

Candescent is the leading cloud-based digital banking solutions provider for financial institutions. We are transforming digital banking with intelligent, cloud-powered solutions that connect account opening, digital banking, and branch experiences for financial institutions. Our advanced technology and developer tools enable seamless, differentiated customer journeys that elevate trust, service, and innovation. Success here requires flexibility in a fast-paced environment, a client-first mindset, and a commitment to delivering consistent, reliable results as part of a performance-driven, values-led team. With team members around the world, Candescent is an equal opportunity employer.

Director of Engineering, Mobile Platform

You thrive in environments where the platform is the product where reliability, developer experience, and extensibility are core features. You’re comfortable navigating build‑vs‑buy decisions, managing technical debt alongside new capabilities, and communicating tradeoffs clearly to executive leadership.

The Opportunity

Candescent is seeking a Director of Engineering, Mobile Platform to lead the evolution of the application foundation that powers our consumer digital banking experience today and will expand into business and commercial banking. This is not a narrow mobile role: you will own the cross‑cutting platform layer that underpins both mobile and web applications.

You will guide the architecture, reliability, and developer experience for the application foundation covering instrumentation, authentication, observability, error handling, logging, telemetry, and plugin infrastructure that product teams rely on to build and ship features.

Key Areas of Responsibility

Platform Architecture & Extensibility


Design and evolve extensibility surfaces including public/internal APIs, event-driven interfaces, webhooks, and plugin endpoints that enable integrations and custom applications.
Develop resilient, production-grade services using modern service frameworks and proven reliability patterns (e.g., retries, circuit breaking, graceful degradation).
Own the full API lifecycle: requirements, design, implementation, versioning, backward compatibility, and deprecation strategy.
Lead gateway engineering: authentication/authorization policies, rate limiting, traffic shaping, caching, and progressive deployment strategies.


Reliability, Observability & Performance


Define and enforce performance and reliability standards across services and APIs, including latency, throughput, error budgets, and SLA/SLO adherence.
Integrate performance and reliability testing into CI/CD workflows.
Establish comprehensive observability practices: structured logging, metrics, distributed tracing, and real-time monitoring.
Drive proactive incident prevention and root-cause analysis through data and instrumentation.


Application Foundation & Developer Experience


Own instrumentation, error handling, logging, and telemetry across mobile and web surfaces.
Lead the evolution of authentication and identity frameworks supporting consumer, business, and future commercial banking users.
Build and maintain the observability stack and performance analytics for the application layer.
Develop plugin architecture and scaffolding that enables product teams to build independently and ship faster.
Deliver high-quality developer documentation, API collections, sandbox environments, and SDKs that reduce time-to-first-call.


Cross-Platform Product Expansion


Define the platform strategy as Candescent expands from consumer into business and commercial banking.
Ensure the application foundation supports both native mobile and web delivery models.
Partner with Product, Design, and peer engineering leaders to define the technical roadmap and API contracts.
Lead the transition from monolithic patterns to modular, plugin-driven architecture.


Global Team Leadership & Scaling


Lead team and engineering managers and their teams across North America, India, and Serbia. Team size 50-70 employees.
Scale the organization while maintaining velocity, quality, and a strong engineering culture.
Establish engineering standards, development workflows, and delivery cadences optimized for distributed teams.
Foster a culture of ownership, technical excellence, and continuous improvement.


Required Experience


Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or equivalent work experience.
8+ years of hands-on experience building and operating production services using modern service frameworks.
Experience with mobile and/or web application development from 0→1, with strong architectural instincts.
Deep expertise in API design, lifecycle management, and platform engineering at scale.
Experience working with full stack and React Native.
Proven success leading and scaling globally distributed engineering organizations.
Strong understanding of authentication/authorization protocols and security best practices.
Experience operating API gateways and managing traffic and security policies.
Demonstrated ability to establish observability practices and drive reliability improvements.
Must be legally authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in digital banking, payments, lending, or other regulated FinTech domains.
Background in platform-as-a-product models, including internal developer experience, SDK design, and self-service tooling.
Experience in PE-backed or high-growth environments undergoing rapid transformation.


Statement to Third Party Agencies

To ALL recruitment agencies: Candescent only accepts resumes from agencies on the preferred supplier list. Please do not forward resumes to our applicant tracking system, Candescent employees, or any Candescent facility. Candescent is not responsible for any fees or charges associated with unsolicited resumes.

coverLetter:
Andrew Phelan and the Moonshot Labs Hiring Team 

 Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex, TX 

 Subject: Application for AI Development Manager 

 Dear Andrew Phelan and the Moonshot Labs Hiring Team, 

 It is with genuine excitement that I submit my application for the AI Development Manager position at Moonshot Labs. Having closely followed your innovative approach to turning AI inward to accelerate manufacturing workflows, I am deeply impressed by your commitment to practical, production-ready AI agents. My journey, spanning over two decades in software engineering and executive leadership, including significant contributions at a global retail leader like 7-Eleven, has uniquely prepared me to lead your software development team in this critical mission. 

 Throughout my career, I have thrived as a player-coach technology executive, adept at making definitive architecture calls, setting the technical bar, and scaling high-performing engineering teams to deliver impactful solutions. My experience at a global retail leader like 7-Eleven involved integrating complex systems and driving operational efficiency across vast operations, skills directly transferable to accelerating manufacturing workflows. My recent focus on advanced AI, including agentic workflows and LLMs – bolstered by my participation in the MIT Agentic AI program – directly aligns with your requirements for building, deploying, and scaling production-ready AI agents. I possess deep expertise in distributed systems, backend architectures, and have consistently delivered through Agile/Scrum methodologies with robust CI/CD pipelines. 

 I am particularly drawn to the opportunity to act as a single point of contact for leaders across Operations, Procurement, and Finance. My career has consistently involved translating complex technical roadmaps into strategic business outcomes, fostering collaborative environments, and navigating shifting priorities in fast-paced enterprise settings. I am passionate about cultivating teams that excel in technical excellence while maintaining a relentless focus on delivering tangible value and operational impact. 

 Thank you for considering my application. I am eager to discuss how my leadership experience and technical prowess in AI engineering can contribute to Moonshot Labs' success in revolutionizing manufacturing with intelligent agents. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi
jobDescription:
AI Development Manager - Moonshot Labs
Location: Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex, TX (Hybrid/Onsite)

About Moonshot Labs:
At Moonshot Labs, we are turning AI inward to accelerate manufacturing workflows from the shop floor to leadership. We build practical, production-ready AI agents and workflows rather than theoretical frameworks.

Role Overview:
We are seeking an AI Development Manager to lead our software development team. You will be a player-coach technology executive who can make definitive architecture calls, set the technical bar, and lead high-scale engineering teams.

Key Responsibilities:
- Manage the roadmap and handle shifting priorities in a fast-paced environment.
- Act as the single point of contact for leaders across Operations, Procurement, and Finance.
- Build, deploy, and scale production-ready AI agents (LLMs, function calling, agentic workflows, retrieval systems).
- Lead a team of software developers using Agile/Scrum sprint structures, CI/CD pipelines, and staging disciplines.

Qualifications:
- 5+ years of engineering management experience, with 10+ years in software engineering.
- Deep expertise in distributed systems, backend architectures, and Agile delivery.
- Hands-on capability and interest in applied AI, LLMs, and agentic workflows.
- Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
- Hiring manager: Andrew Phelan (CTO)

Andrew Phelan and the Moonshot Labs Hiring Team 

 Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex, TX 

 Subject: Application for AI Development Manager 

 Dear Andrew Phelan and the Moonshot Labs Hiring Team, 

 My career journey of over two decades in software engineering, including leading high-impact AI/ML initiatives at organizations like 7-Eleven and CVS Health, has uniquely prepared me to embrace the challenges and opportunities of the AI Development Manager role at Moonshot Labs. I am particularly inspired by your mission to turn AI inward to accelerate manufacturing workflows, a practical application of AI that resonates deeply with my experience in deploying production-ready agentic workflows and LLM applications at scale. 

 Throughout my tenure, I've served as a player-coach technology executive, adept at making definitive architecture calls, setting the technical bar, and guiding high-scale engineering teams through complex roadmaps and shifting priorities. My expertise spans building and scaling distributed systems, backend architectures, and implementing rigorous Agile/Scrum and CI/CD pipelines—all critical for developing robust, scalable AI solutions from the shop floor to leadership, as envisioned by Moonshot Labs. My recent specialization, including the MIT Agentic AI program, further solidifies my hands-on capability and interest in applied AI, LLMs, and agentic workflows. 

 What truly excites me about Moonshot Labs is the emphasis on practical, production-ready AI agents. My background includes acting as a single point of contact for leaders across diverse functions, a skill I honed in dynamic environments, and I am confident in my ability to bridge technical vision with operational and financial realities. I am eager to bring my blend of leadership, technical depth, and passion for innovation to your team, contributing to the development of transformative AI solutions for manufacturing. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

coverLetter:
Hiring Team Candescent Atlanta, GA 

 Subject: Application for Director Engineering, Mobile Platform 

 Dear Candescent Hiring Team, 

 It is with great enthusiasm that I submit my application for the Director Engineering, Mobile Platform role at Candescent. With over two decades of experience leading high-performing engineering teams and architecting robust, scalable platforms, I am particularly drawn to Candescent's mission of transforming digital banking through intelligent, cloud-powered solutions that connect and elevate customer journeys. My journey has equipped me with a deep understanding of what it takes to build and evolve complex application foundations that underpin critical consumer and business experiences. 

 My background, including leadership roles at companies like 7-Eleven and CVS Health, has provided me with extensive experience in leading the development of large-scale, customer-facing platforms. At 7-Eleven, I was deeply involved in building systems that required immense reliability, performance, and an exceptional developer experience for rapid feature delivery across diverse consumer touchpoints. While my recent focus has included advanced AI and agentic systems, the underlying principles of architecting resilient services, managing API lifecycles, establishing comprehensive observability, and fostering innovation in fast-paced environments are directly transferable to evolving Candescent's mobile and web application foundation into business and commercial banking. 

 I am confident in my ability to guide the architecture, reliability, and developer experience for your core platform layer. My expertise spans designing extensibility surfaces, owning the full API lifecycle, leading gateway engineering for authentication and authorization, and defining robust observability practices. Furthermore, my proven track record in scaling globally distributed engineering organizations and fostering a culture of technical excellence aligns perfectly with your goals of expanding velocity, quality, and ownership across North America, India, and Serbia. I am particularly excited by the opportunity to lead the transition from monolithic patterns to a modular, plugin-driven architecture that empowers product teams to build independently. 

 Candescent's commitment to a client-first mindset and delivering consistent, reliable results resonates deeply with my own values. I am eager to contribute my leadership and technical acumen to your team, helping to define the platform strategy as you expand into new banking sectors and continue to innovate. Thank you for considering my application; I look forward to discussing how my experience can directly benefit Candescent's continued success. 

 Sincerely, 

 Bhagath Siddi

Candescent
